1.label: Stop / Check
1.meaning: Drug-class active or toxic metal. Treat seriously — investigate or avoid. Often hides in lightening/brightening/whitening products and may be undeclared.

2.label: Worth Reducing
2.meaning: Endocrine-disruptor or sensitizer concern. Not acutely dangerous; risk is cumulative, low-dose, and reversible. Fine occasionally; prefer alternatives for daily leave-on use.

3.label: All Clear
3.meaning: Benign / beneficial. Standard skincare ingredient with no notable safety concern at cosmetic levels.

meta.policy: When a substance is uncertain or evidence is mixed, it is sorted into the MORE severe tier and marked precautionary:true.

ingredients.name: 1,4-Dioxane
ingredients.note: A likely human carcinogen formed as a byproduct of ethoxylation (e.g., in sulfates, PEGs, polysorbates) and never intentionally added or listed on labels. New York and California cap residual levels; minimize via vacuum-stripped ingredients.

ingredients.name: Arsenic
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 human carcinogen found as a contaminant in some lightening creams and pigmented cosmetics. No safe cosmetic level; chronic exposure causes skin cancer and arsenical keratosis.

ingredients.name: Asbestos (Talc Contaminant)
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen that co-occurs geologically with talc and has been repeatedly detected in talc-based powders and makeup, prompting recalls. Linked to mesothelioma and ovarian cancer; a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Benzene
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen found as a contamination/degradation byproduct (notably in aerosols and benzoyl-peroxide products), triggering major recalls. No safe level; a hard stop in any cosmetic.

ingredients.name: Beryllium
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen and potent sensitizer that can occur as a trace contaminant in mineral-derived pigments and talc. No acceptable cosmetic use; a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Betamethasone
ingredients.note: Prescription corticosteroid; flag if present in a product not sold as a medicated treatment.

ingredients.name: Bimatoprost
ingredients.note: Prescription prostaglandin analogue (Latisse). Same risk profile as isopropyl cloprostenate; regulated as a drug.

ingredients.name: Cadmium
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen and nephrotoxin found as a trace contaminant in pigments and lightening products. Restricted in the EU; treat any disclosed presence as a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Calamine
ingredients.note: FDA-recognized OTC skin-protectant drug (zinc oxide plus ~0.5% ferric oxide) with anti-itch/astringent action. Drug-class active, listed in WHO essential medicines.

ingredients.name: Clindamycin
ingredients.note: Prescription antibiotic found undeclared in 'anti-acne' creams; misuse drives antibiotic resistance and can trigger colitis. Not a legal cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Clobetasol propionate
ingredients.note: Potent prescription corticosteroid sometimes found undeclared in 'whitening' or 'miracle' creams. Long-term facial use causes skin thinning, rebound dermatitis, telangiectasia.

ingredients.name: Dechloro Dihydroxy Difluoro Ethylcloprostenolamide
ingredients.note: Marketed as a 'gentler' lash active to dodge prostaglandin labeling. Precautionary Tier 1: structurally prostaglandin-adjacent, real-world side-effect reports mirror prostaglandins, evidence of safety is thin. Treat as a drug-class active until proven otherwise.

ingredients.name: Desonide
ingredients.note: Low-potency prescription corticosteroid still drug-classed and not permitted undeclared in cosmetics; turns up in adulterated lightening products. Chronic facial use causes steroid dermatitis.

ingredients.name: Desoximetasone
ingredients.note: Potent prescription corticosteroid found in adulterated lightening creams. Not a legal cosmetic ingredient; chronic use causes atrophy and steroid acne.

ingredients.name: Dexamethasone
ingredients.note: Prescription corticosteroid detected undeclared in fairness creams and 'beauty' products; systemic absorption risks adrenal suppression. Any presence in a cosmetic is a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Diflucortolone Valerate
ingredients.note: Potent prescription steroid commonly identified in seized skin-lightening creams in Asia and the Middle East. Undeclared use is a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Fluocinolone Acetonide
ingredients.note: Prescription corticosteroid found in adulterated lightening creams, often co-formulated with hydroquinone and tretinoin in unregulated 'tri-creams'. Not legal as an undeclared cosmetic ingredient; treat as a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Fluocinonide
ingredients.note: A potent prescription corticosteroid repeatedly detected undeclared in OTC skin-lightening creams; chronic use causes skin atrophy, steroid rosacea, and HPA-axis suppression. Any alias is a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Fluticasone Propionate
ingredients.note: Potent prescription corticosteroid found in unregulated topical creams. Drug-classed and illegal as an undeclared cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Halobetasol Propionate
ingredients.note: Super-high-potency prescription steroid (class I) flagged in adulterated bleaching creams. Undisclosed use carries serious atrophy and systemic absorption risk; a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Halometasone
ingredients.note: Potent halogenated prescription corticosteroid identified in adulterated whitening and anti-fungal creams. Undeclared cosmetic use is a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Hexavalent Chromium
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen and potent skin sensitizer found as a contaminant in pigments and tattoo/cosmetic colorants. Banned in EU cosmetics above trace limits; a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Hydrocortisone
ingredients.note: Mild OTC steroid — legitimate for short-term medicated use, but precautionary-flagged here because daily long-term facial use in a non-medicated product is inappropriate and causes skin thinning.

ingredients.name: Hydroquinone
ingredients.note: Skin-lightening drug, not legal for OTC cosmetic sale in the US. Linked to rashes, facial swelling, and permanent skin discoloration (ochronosis). The active in many illegal 'whitening' creams.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl cloprostenate
ingredients.note: Synthetic prostaglandin analogue — the lash-serum active. Drug class derived from glaucoma medication. Can cause periorbital fat loss (sunken eyes), iris pigmentation, conjunctival changes, orbital irritation. Some effects permanent.

ingredients.name: Ketoconazole
ingredients.note: Azole antifungal drug used in anti-dandruff/seborrheic shampoos; a regulated topical active, not a cosmetic ingredient, and an occasional contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Latanoprost
ingredients.note: Prostaglandin analogue glaucoma drug; appears in some lash serums. Same periorbital risk profile.

ingredients.name: Lead acetate
ingredients.note: Historically in progressive hair dyes / some imported cosmetics. Cumulative neurotoxin; banned in cosmetics in many jurisdictions.

ingredients.name: Mercury
ingredients.note: Toxic heavy metal, frequently UNLABELED in lightening creams/soaps. Absorbs through skin; can damage nervous, digestive and immune systems, lungs, kidneys, skin, eyes. Any alias is a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Methyl Methacrylate
ingredients.note: Acrylic nail monomer the FDA deems adulterating and that 30+ US states ban; causes severe contact allergy, nail-plate loss, and permanent fingertip numbness.

ingredients.name: Methyl Nicotinate
ingredients.note: Drug-class rubefacient/peripheral vasodilator that induces dermal blood flow and erythema. Causes transient irritation; should not be used on broken skin.

ingredients.name: Methylmercury
ingredients.note: Organomercury; neurotoxic. Hard stop in any leave-on facial product.

ingredients.name: Minoxidil
ingredients.note: Topical OTC/prescription drug for androgenetic alopecia, not a cosmetic; can cause scalp irritation, unwanted facial hair, and rarely cardiovascular effects.

ingredients.name: Mometasone Furoate
ingredients.note: Potent prescription corticosteroid detected in unregulated skin-lightening and fairness creams across multiple FDA and international seizures. A hard stop in any cosmetic.

ingredients.name: Monobenzone
ingredients.note: Prescription depigmenting drug that causes permanent, irreversible, often disfiguring destruction of melanocytes and confetti-like depigmentation far beyond the treated area. Approved only for extensive vitiligo, never for cosmetic lightening; a hard stop.

ingredients.name: Monomethyl Ether of Hydroquinone
ingredients.note: A depigmenting agent and skin sensitizer that can cause permanent leukoderma; used as a drug (with tretinoin) and as a polymerization inhibitor, not a safe cosmetic lightener.

ingredients.name: Neomycin
ingredients.note: Prescription aminoglycoside antibiotic and one of the most common topical contact allergens; undeclared use in creams risks sensitization and cross-reactivity. A hard stop in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Nitrosamines
ingredients.note: Carcinogenic byproducts (notably NDELA) that form when amines like DEA/TEA react with nitrosating agents in formulas; IARC classifies several as probable human carcinogens. EU bans nitrosamine-forming combinations.

ingredients.name: PFAS (Per- and Polyfluoroalkyl Substances)
ingredients.note: Persistent 'forever chemicals' used for slip in long-wear makeup and detected as contaminants; linked to cancer, thyroid and immune harm. Banned in cosmetics in several US states and the EU.

ingredients.name: Teprenone
ingredients.note: Approved oral pharmaceutical (gastric anti-ulcer / mucosal protectant, Selbex) repurposed topically as an anti-aging active; a heat-shock-protein inducer. Drug-class active, no CIR assessment, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Tretinoin
ingredients.note: Prescription-strength retinoid — effective and dermatologist-prescribed, but precautionary Tier 1 in a self-care context: drug-strength, teratogenic (must avoid in pregnancy), not for casual influencer-driven use without medical oversight.

ingredients.name: Triamcinolone Acetonide
ingredients.note: Mid-to-high potency prescription steroid identified in counterfeit and adulterated 'miracle' creams. Undisclosed use risks atrophy, telangiectasia, and rebound dermatitis.

ingredients.name: 2-Nitro-p-Phenylenediamine
ingredients.note: Nitro-substituted oxidative/direct hair dye and recognized contact sensitizer cross-reacting with PPD; SCCS-restricted with concentration limits.

ingredients.name: 4-Amino-2-Hydroxytoluene
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye coupler and contact allergen; SCCS-assessed and concentration-restricted in permanent hair colorants.

ingredients.name: Abies Sibirica Oil
ingredients.note: Siberian fir needle essential oil containing limonene and pinenes; fragrance allergen exposure with skin-sensitization risk especially when oxidized. Used mainly for scent.

sources.label: PubChem: Abies sibirica Leaf Oil compound record
PMC: Identification of Abies sibirica Polyprenols and Characterisation of Polyprenol-Containing Liposomes
PubMed: Preparation and evaluation of oral gel containing essential oil from Siberian fir (Abies sibirica)

ingredients.name: Acetonitrile
ingredients.note: Solvent in some artificial-nail glue removers; metabolized to cyanide with delayed toxicity, implicated in pediatric poisoning deaths.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Octapeptide-3
ingredients.note: Synthetic SNAP-25-derived neuromodulating peptide marketed to soften expression lines; mechanism mimics botulinum signaling with limited independent safety data, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-2
ingredients.note: Acetylated synthetic tetrapeptide marketed as a firming/elastin-supporting active mimicking thymic factors; bioactive claims with limited independent safety data, so precautionary.

sources.label: PubChem compound record for Acetyl tetrapeptide-2 (CID 91936905)
PubMed: The tetrapeptide N-acetyl-Pro-Pro-Tyr-Leu in skin care (AcSDKP regulatory role)
PubMed: Regulatory role of the tetrapeptide AcSDKP in skin and hair physiology

ingredients.name: Achillea Millefolium Extract
ingredients.note: Yarrow extract used for soothing/conditioning. As an Asteraceae (Compositae) plant it contains sesquiterpene lactones that can cause contact allergy in sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Achillea Millefolium Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Yarrow (Asteraceae) extract containing sesquiterpene lactones (e.g. alpha-peroxyachifolid), recognized contact sensitizers in Compositae-allergic individuals.

ingredients.name: Alpha-Isomethyl Ionone
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a violet-orris note used widely in perfumery. A listed contact sensitizer of concern for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Glycinate
ingredients.note: Aluminum salt of glycine (an antacid) used in cosmetics as a buffering/absorbent agent. Aluminum salts carry residual neurotoxicity/accumulation concern, so flagged Tier 2 precautionary.

ingredients.name: Ammonia
ingredients.note: Alkaline swelling/oxidizing agent in permanent hair color; corrosive and a strong respiratory and eye irritant at use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Laureth Sulfate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated sulfate that can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless stripped during manufacture. Generally milder than non-ethoxylated sulfates but still potentially drying with frequent use.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Persulfate
ingredients.note: Persulfate bleach booster causing occupational asthma, rhinitis, and both irritant and allergic contact dermatitis in hairdressers.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Thioglycolate
ingredients.note: Reducing agent in perms and thio relaxers; a skin/eye irritant and contact allergen with a strong odor and SCCS concentration limits.

ingredients.name: Amyl Cinnamal
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a jasmine note used in floral fragrances. A listed contact sensitizer relevant for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Amylcinnamyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a floral balsamic odor. A listed contact sensitizer of concern for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Amyris Balsamifera Bark Oil
ingredients.note: Woody sesquiterpene-rich oil used as a fragrance and sandalwood substitute/fixative. Generally low-irritant but, as a fragrance essential oil, carries precautionary sensitization potential.

sources.label: PubChem: Amyris balsamifera oil compound record
PMC: Essential Oils as Alternative to Pyrethroids Resistance against Anopheles Species (includes Amyris balsamifera)
PMC: Identification of Essential Oils with Strong Activity against Stationary Phase Borrelia burgdorferi

ingredients.name: Anise Alcohol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a sweet floral-anise odor. A listed contact sensitizer relevant for fragrance-reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Antimony
ingredients.note: Toxic metal found as a contaminant in eye and lip cosmetics and historic kohl; antimony trioxide is an IARC Group 2A possible carcinogen. EU sets strict trace limits.

ingredients.name: Arbutin
ingredients.note: Gentle brightening agent that can slowly convert a small fraction to hydroquinone. At cosmetic levels considered low-risk — precautionary Tier 2 only because of the hydroquinone connection; nothing like the Tier-1 lightening drugs.

ingredients.name: Arnica Montana Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Arnica flowers contain sesquiterpene lactones reported to cause contact dermatitis; CIR found data insufficient and sensitization evidence mixed.

ingredients.name: Aroma/Flavor
ingredients.note: Undisclosed flavor/aroma blend (per 21 CFR 701.3) that can conceal multiple potential fragrance allergens. Composition is a trade secret.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Vulgaris Oil
ingredients.note: Mugwort essential oil containing sesquiterpene lactones; potential allergen especially for Asteraceae-sensitive individuals, with caution advised in pregnancy.

ingredients.name: Backhousia Citriodora Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon myrtle leaf extract is exceptionally high in citral (90-98%), a known fragrance allergen/skin sensitizer. Treated as a botanical sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Barosma Betulina Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Buchu leaf extract contains pulegone (2-8%), a hepatotoxic monoterpene, plus diosphenol; can cause irritation/sensitization. Treated as a botanical sensitizer/toxicant concern.

ingredients.name: Benzalkonium Chloride
ingredients.note: Quaternary ammonium preservative and known irritant/sensitizer, especially on mucosa and eyes. EU restricts cosmetic use (up to 0.1% as preservative).

ingredients.name: Benzethonium Chloride
ingredients.note: Cationic surfactant antimicrobial that can irritate skin and eyes and occasionally sensitize. EU limits leave-on use to 0.1% and prohibits it in oral products.

ingredients.name: Benzisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Isothiazolinone-class contact allergen and frequent cause of allergic contact dermatitis. Not approved as a cosmetic preservative in the EU; appears mainly via industrial/raw-material carryover.

ingredients.name: Benzophenone
ingredients.note: Benzophenone family; endocrine-disruptor class, some members also flagged for carcinogenicity concern.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen also used as a preservative and solvent; a relatively weak but listed sensitizer. Concern is modest and mainly for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Benzoate
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen also used as a fragrance fixative and solvent. A relatively weak listed sensitizer at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Cinnamate
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen found in balsams such as Peru balsam and tolu. A recognized fragrance sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Glycol
ingredients.note: Aromatic glycol used as a preservative/solvent; structurally related to benzyl alcohol, a recognized contact sensitizer, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Benzyl Salicylate
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen used as a fragrance fixative and mild UV absorber. A listed contact sensitizer of concern for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Benzylparaben
ingredients.note: EU-banned paraben ester.

ingredients.name: Bergamot Oil
ingredients.note: Strongly phototoxic due to bergapten (5-MOP); expressed oil is IFRA-limited to ~0.4% on sun-exposed skin unless furocoumarin-free (FCF).

ingredients.name: BHA
ingredients.note: Synthetic antioxidant flagged by some bodies as a possible human carcinogen and endocrine disruptor. Note: NOT the same as 'BHA' meaning salicylic-acid exfoliant — context matters; this entry is the preservative butylated hydroxyanisole.

ingredients.name: BHT
ingredients.note: Synthetic antioxidant with weaker endocrine-disruptor signals than BHA; precautionary.

ingredients.name: Bronopol
ingredients.note: Formaldehyde releaser; can also form nitrosamines.

ingredients.name: Butylparaben
ingredients.note: One of the more potent estrogenic parabens.

ingredients.name: Butylphenyl Methylpropional
ingredients.note: Former EU-26 allergen banned in EU cosmetics from March 2022 as a CMR (reprotoxic) substance, so it should no longer appear in compliant products. Also a contact sensitizer; precautionary flag is false because it is outright prohibited.

ingredients.name: Calendula Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Asteraceae (Compositae) family extract; soothing but can cause contact dermatitis in Compositae-allergic individuals (sesquiterpene lactones).

ingredients.name: Camphor
ingredients.note: Plasticizer in nail polish; an irritant and neurotoxic if ingested, prompting reformulation in many '3-free' and '5-free' products.

ingredients.name: Cananga Odorata Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Ylang-ylang flower extract; a recognized fragrance sensitizer containing EU-declarable allergens (linalool, benzyl benzoate, geraniol) and IFRA-restricted.

ingredients.name: Capryloyl Salicylic Acid
ingredients.note: Lipophilic salicylic acid ester (LHA) used as an exfoliating/keratolytic active; a recent CIR re-review found data insufficient and salicylates carry irritation/photosensitivity cautions. Tier raised for uncertainty.

ingredients.name: Capsicum Frutescens Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Chili pepper extract containing capsaicin; a stimulant that can cause burning/irritation. CIR deems it safe only when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Carbon Black
ingredients.note: Intense black pigment used in eyeliner and mascara. Carries polycyclic aromatic hydrocarbon (PAH) contamination concerns; EU restricts purity and it is banned in cosmetics in some jurisdictions, with possible-carcinogen classification of the substance.

ingredients.name: Ceteareth-20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated fatty-alcohol emulsifier common in creams. Safe as used but ethoxylated, so possible trace 1,4-dioxane prompts precaution.

ingredients.name: Cetrimonium Bromide
ingredients.note: Cationic antimicrobial and conditioning agent that can irritate skin and eyes and cause contact allergy at higher levels. EU restricts concentration depending on rinse-off versus leave-on use.

ingredients.name: Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Extract
ingredients.note: Chamomile extract, generally soothing/anti-inflammatory but an Asteraceae-family plant that can sensitize (sesquiterpene lactones, anthecotulide). CIR found it safe as used.

ingredients.name: Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Flower Oil
ingredients.note: German chamomile flower essential oil (bisabolol/chamazulene); CIR deems chamomile oil safe, but Asteraceae-family sesquiterpene-lactone allergens (e.g., anthecotulide in some sources) pose sensitization risk. Marked precautionary as an aromatic oil.

ingredients.name: Chlorhexidine Digluconate
ingredients.note: Potent antiseptic with rare but documented risk of serious immediate hypersensitivity (including anaphylaxis). EU restricts cosmetic use to 0.3% (as chlorhexidine).

ingredients.name: CI 12085 (Red 36)
ingredients.note: Synthetic monoazo red pigment, FDA-approved for external/cosmetic use at restricted concentration (<=3%) and not permitted in hair dyes. Azo colorant flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: CI 15850 (Red 6)
ingredients.note: Synthetic monoazo red dye, FDA-approved for drug/cosmetic use; not permitted around the eyes. SCCS found it non-sensitizing but azo colorant flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: CI 15850 (Red 7 Lake)
ingredients.note: Calcium lake of the monoazo dye Red 6/7, FDA-approved for drug/cosmetic use; not permitted around the eyes. Azo colorant flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: CI 15985 (Yellow 5)
ingredients.note: CI 15985 is Sunset Yellow FCF (Yellow 6, not Yellow 5); a petroleum azo dye that may cause skin/eye irritation and allergic skin reactions in sensitive users. Name label appears mismatched.

ingredients.name: CI 15985/Yellow 6, Yellow 6 Lake
ingredients.note: Sunset Yellow FCF azo dye and its aluminum lake; FDA-approved colorant but a petroleum azo dye that can cause skin/eye irritation and contact allergy in sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: CI 17200 (Red 33 Lake)
ingredients.note: Water-soluble monoazo red dye lake, FDA-approved for restricted cosmetic use (lipstick <=3%) and not for eye area. Azo colorant flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: CI 17200 (Red 33)
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo dye, FDA-permitted for cosmetics with restrictions (not for eye area; lipstick limit 3%). Can sensitize susceptible individuals, so treated as Tier 2 precautionary.

sources.label: PubChem: D&C Red No. 33 compound record
PMC: Preparative separation and identification of subsidiary colors of D&C Red No. 33 using spiral high-speed counter-current chromatography

ingredients.name: CI 19140 (Yellow 5)
ingredients.note: Tartrazine, a synthetic azo yellow dye; among the azo colorants most associated with hives/intolerance, especially in aspirin-sensitive and asthmatic individuals.

ingredients.name: CI 45380 (Red 22 Lake)
ingredients.note: Xanthene (eosin-type) red dye lake, FDA-certified for external drug/cosmetic and lipstick use but restricted from the eye area; eosins can be photosensitizing. Flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: CI 45380/Red 21
ingredients.note: Xanthene coal-tar dye (D&C Red 21/22); FDA-certified for lips/external cosmetics but restricted from the eye area, with potential for skin/eye irritation in sensitive users.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – D&C Red No. 21

ingredients.name: CI 45410/Red 27, Red 27 Lake, Red 28 Lake
ingredients.note: Xanthene coal-tar dye (D&C Red 27/28) and its lakes; FDA-approved for lips/external use but not the eye area, with potential for skin and eye irritation.

sources.label: PubChem: D&C Red No. 27 compound record (CID 83511)
FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – D&C Red No. 22 (related lake reference)

ingredients.name: CI 73360/Red 30, Red 30 Lake
ingredients.note: Thioindigoid coal-tar pigment (D&C Red 30) and its lake; FDA-approved for cosmetics including lips but not the eye area, with possible mild irritation. Coal-tar origin warrants caution.

sources.label: PubChem: D&C Red No. 30 compound record (CID 3000709)
PubMed: Identification and separation of impurity in thioindigoid color additive D&C Red No. 30
FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – D&C Red No. 30

ingredients.name: CI 75470 (Carmine)
ingredients.note: Insect-derived red pigment approved for cosmetic use but a known cause of allergic/hypersensitivity reactions, including severe responses in sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Cinchona Pubescens Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Cinchona bark extract containing quinine alkaloids and tannins, used as an astringent scalp/skin-conditioning botanical; quinine is a recognized contact sensitizer/photosensitizer and the bark is pharmacologically active. Treated as a precautionary sensitizer botanical.

ingredients.name: Cinnamal
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen and the main odor of cinnamon bark; one of the stronger fragrance sensitizers. IFRA-restricted and of clear concern for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Cinnamomum Cassia Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Cassia (cinnamon) bark extract containing cinnamaldehyde and eugenol, moderate skin sensitizers; EU-declarable allergens.

ingredients.name: Cinnamomum Cassia Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Cinnamaldehyde-rich essential oil; a recognized contact and photo-allergen capable of allergic contact dermatitis. Treated as a sensitizer.

sources.label: PMC: Safety and efficacy of essential oil from Cinnamomum cassia for use in animal species (EFSA assessment)
PMC: Cinnamomum cassia Presl: A Review of Traditional Uses, Phytochemistry, Pharmacology and Toxicology
PubMed: The essential oil from twigs of Cinnamomum cassia Presl alleviates pain and inflammation in mice

ingredients.name: Cinnamon Bark Oil
ingredients.note: Potent dermal irritant and sensitizer driven by cinnamaldehyde; restricted in fragrance use and a common patch-test positive.

ingredients.name: Cinnamyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen found in cinnamon, balsams and hyacinth notes. A moderate fragrance sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Cinoxate
ingredients.note: Cinnamate UVB filter that the FDA has not found GRASE due to insufficient safety data, with absorption and endocrine questions shared by other cinnamates. Rarely used in modern formulas.

ingredients.name: Citral
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen giving a strong lemon scent (a mix of geranial and neral). A fairly potent sensitizer, usually quenched with other materials in formula.

ingredients.name: Citronella Oil
ingredients.note: High geraniol/citronellol content makes it a notable fragrance sensitizer; used in insect-repellent cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Citronellol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a rose-citrus scent, common in florals and rose-type fragrances. A moderate contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Citrus / Lemon Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed citrus peel oils are phototoxic from furocoumarins and contain limonene, a frequent allergen; IFRA restricts levels on sun-exposed skin.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantifolia (Lime) Juice
ingredients.note: Lime fruit juice used as a botanical skin conditioner; lime is a classic cause of phytophotodermatitis via furocoumarins and is acidic. Treated as precautionary phototoxin/sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Bergamia (Bergamot) Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Expressed bergamot oil is phototoxic due to furocoumarins (bergapten/5-MOP) under UVA; documented phototoxic reactions. Use FCF/furocoumarin-reduced grades and avoid sun.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Orange flower extract carrying citrus essential-oil constituents including the established fragrance allergen limonene. Citrus constituents are listed as contact allergens by the EU SCCS.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Juice
ingredients.note: Sweet orange fruit juice used as a skin-conditioning botanical; citrus-derived material can be mildly acidic and potentially phototoxic/sensitizing. Treated as precautionary.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Oil
ingredients.note: Sweet orange essential oil high in limonene (a labeled fragrance allergen) with furanocoumarin-related phototoxicity. CIR restricts 5-MOP content.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Peel Oil
ingredients.note: Sweet orange peel essential oil is high in limonene (a frequent skin sensitizer) and contains furanocoumarins that can be mildly phototoxic. CIR sets a 5-MOP limit; treated as a sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Citrus Grandis (Grapefruit) Peel Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed grapefruit peel oil contains furocoumarins (phototoxic) and oxidizable limonene, a recognized sensitizer. Flagged Tier 2 precautionary for phototoxicity and sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Citrus Junos Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Yuzu fruit extract used as antioxidant/skin conditioner; contains limonene and linalool, which are recognized fragrance allergens.

sources.label: PubMed: Preparation, Characterization, and Biological Activities of Topical Anti-Aging Ingredients in Citrus junos Callus Extract
PMC: Anti-aging ingredients in Citrus junos Callus Extract (full PMC version)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Citrus Fruit-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Citrus Junos Peel Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed yuzu peel oil used as a fragrance and skin-conditioning agent; it is low in phototoxic furocoumarins relative to other citrus peel oils but as a citrus peel essential oil still carries fragrance-allergen/photosensitization potential. Treated as a precautionary fragrance ingredient.

ingredients.name: Citrus Latifolia Peel Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed lime peel oil contains phototoxic furocoumarins and can cause phytophotodermatitis; IFRA/CIR restrict leave-on use (lime oil expressed limit ~0.7%). Treated as a precautionary sensitizer/phototoxin.

ingredients.name: Citrus Limon (Lemon) Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed lemon oil contains furocoumarins (phototoxic) and limonene that oxidizes into known skin sensitizers. Flagged Tier 2 precautionary for phototoxicity and sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Citrus Limon (Lemon) Juice
ingredients.note: Lemon fruit juice used as a botanical skin conditioner; it is acidic and citrus-derived material carries furocoumarin-linked photosensitivity and irritation potential. Treated as precautionary.

ingredients.name: Citrus Limon (Lemon) Peel Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed lemon peel oil containing limonene and furanocoumarins; phototoxic and a recognized sensitizer. Use restricted and UV exposure avoided after application.

ingredients.name: Citrus Paradisi (Grapefruit) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Grapefruit fruit extract used for antioxidant skin conditioning, but furanocoumarin content may increase photosensitivity and cause irritation in sensitive skin. Treated as precautionary phototoxin/sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Citrus Unshiu Peel Extract
ingredients.note: Satsuma mandarin peel extract rich in flavonoids used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical; citrus peel can carry furocoumarin-related photosensitivity. Treated as precautionary.

ingredients.name: Climbazole
ingredients.note: Imidazole antifungal used as an anti-dandruff and preservative agent with potential endocrine-activity flags under SCCS review. EU restricts concentration by product type.

ingredients.name: Clove Oil
ingredients.note: 80-95% eugenol, a recognized R43 sensitizer; frequent cause of allergic contact dermatitis and cross-reacts with cinnamon oils.

ingredients.name: Cobalt
ingredients.note: A frequent skin sensitizer found as a trace contaminant in pigments and dark cosmetics, often co-occurring with nickel allergy. Inhaled cobalt is an IARC Group 2A carcinogen.

ingredients.name: Cocamide MEA
ingredients.note: Foam stabilizer considered safer than the DEA version but it can still carry trace free monoethanolamine and form nitrosamines if combined with nitrosating agents. Generally low risk in finished, properly formulated products.

ingredients.name: Cocamidopropyl Betaine
ingredients.note: Mild secondary surfactant and foam booster named Allergen of the Year 2004; it is a notable contact sensitizer. Reactions are usually driven by manufacturing impurities (amidoamine and dimethylaminopropylamine), so well-purified grades are far less sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Copaifera Officinalis (Balsam Copaiba) Resin
ingredients.note: Copaiba balsam resin rich in terpenes; as a balsam/tree resin it can cause allergic contact dermatitis in balsam-sensitive individuals. Treated as a Tier 2 sensitizer concern.

sources.label: PubChem: Copaifera officinalis whole compound record
PMC: Evaluation of biological activities of Copaiba (Copaifera spp): a comprehensive review
PMC: Chemistry and Biological Activities of Terpenoids from Copaiba (Copaifera spp.) Oleoresins

ingredients.name: Coumarin
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a sweet hay scent, found in tonka bean and many fragrances. A moderate contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Cyclohexasiloxane (D6)
ingredients.note: EU REACH restricts D6 to <0.1% in wash-off (June 2026) and leave-on (June 2027) products as a PBT/vPvB SVHC. Concern is environmental persistence and bioaccumulation rather than acute skin risk.

ingredients.name: Cyclomethicone
ingredients.note: Generic name for mixtures of cyclic siloxanes (often D4/D5/D6), all now EU-restricted as PBT/vPvB substances of very high concern. Reformulation has largely moved toward linear silicones because of these restrictions.

ingredients.name: Cyclomethicone (D4/D5 Blend)
ingredients.note: Trade blends labeled cyclomethicone contain restricted cyclic siloxanes (D4 and/or D5), now subject to EU REACH limits as PBT/vPvB SVHCs. Concern is environmental persistence plus D4 reprotoxicity; reformulation toward linear silicones is ongoing.

ingredients.name: Cyclopentasiloxane (D5)
ingredients.note: EU REACH restricts D5 to <0.1% in wash-off (June 2026) and leave-on (June 2027) products as a PBT/vPvB Substance of Very High Concern. Environmental persistence and bioaccumulation drive the concern; skin tolerance is generally good.

ingredients.name: Cyclotetrasiloxane (D4)
ingredients.note: EU-classified SVHC (PBT/vPvB) and reprotoxic (suspected endocrine disruptor); REACH restricts it to <0.1% in cosmetics. The most concerning of the cyclic siloxanes due to reproductive toxicity in addition to environmental persistence.

ingredients.name: Cymbopogon Martini Oil
ingredients.note: Palmarosa essential oil rich in geraniol, an EU-listed fragrance allergen; can sensitize/irritate susceptible skin.

ingredients.name: D&C Orange No. 5
ingredients.note: Brominated fluorescein dye used in lipsticks with strict FDA limits. Coal-tar-derived with sensitization/photosensitization concerns; not for the eye area.

ingredients.name: D&C Red No. 21
ingredients.note: Brominated fluorescein (xanthene) dye used as a staining lip color. Coal-tar-derived with possible photosensitization/sensitization concerns; not for eye-area use.

ingredients.name: D&C Red No. 27
ingredients.note: Halogenated fluorescein dye used in long-wear lip stains. Coal-tar-derived xanthene with sensitization/photosensitization concerns; prohibited near the eyes.

ingredients.name: D&C Red No. 33
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo dye permitted for external use and lips with limits; not allowed in the eye area. Coal-tar/azo-dye sensitization concerns apply.

ingredients.name: D&C Red No. 6
ingredients.note: Sodium salt azo dye used in lip and face makeup. Coal-tar/azo origin with sensitization potential; FDA prohibits use near the eyes.

ingredients.name: D&C Red No. 7 Lake
ingredients.note: Calcium lake of an azo dye widely used in lipstick and blush. Coal-tar/azo-dye origin with sensitization concerns; approved for external use but not the eye area.

ingredients.name: D&C Yellow No. 10
ingredients.note: Quinoline (coal-tar-derived) dye approved for external cosmetics and limited lip use. Sensitization concerns; not approved for the eye area by FDA.

ingredients.name: Di(2-ethylhexyl) phthalate
ingredients.note: Endocrine-disruptor phthalate; restricted in many jurisdictions.

ingredients.name: Di-HEMA Trimethylhexyl Dicarbamate
ingredients.note: HEMA-derived urethane methacrylate in gel polishes; a notable contact sensitizer, especially from incompletely cured product.

ingredients.name: Diazolidinyl urea
ingredients.note: Formaldehyde releaser; sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Dibutyl phthalate
ingredients.note: Reproductive/developmental toxicant; restricted in the EU and several US states.

ingredients.name: Diethyl phthalate
ingredients.note: Endocrine disruptor; commonly carried inside 'fragrance.' Linked to reproductive/developmental concerns. Clears the body fast once exposure stops.

ingredients.name: Dipeptide Diaminobutyroyl Benzylamide Diacetate
ingredients.note: Synthetic peptide (SYN-AKE) mimicking Waglerin-1 viper venom; an nicotinic acetylcholine receptor antagonist marketed for muscle-relaxing wrinkle reduction. Neuroactive mechanism warrants precaution.

sources.label: PubChem: Dipeptide diaminobutyroyl benzylamide diacetate (Syn-ake) compound record (CID 71465152)
PubMed: Effect of a serum containing acetyl hexapeptide-8 and dipeptide diaminobutyroyl benzylamide diacetate on skin biomarkers and wrinkles

ingredients.name: Disodium Laureth Sulfosuccinate
ingredients.note: Mild secondary surfactant that is itself gentle, but being ethoxylated it can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless stripped during manufacture. The contaminant, not the surfactant, is the only notable concern.

ingredients.name: DMDM hydantoin
ingredients.note: Slowly releases formaldehyde. Common sensitizer; flag for fragrance-sensitive or reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Enzacamene
ingredients.note: UVB camphor filter with consistent animal and in vitro endocrine-disruption data (estrogenic and thyroid effects). Banned in the EU as of 2025 and not approved by the FDA.

ingredients.name: Escin
ingredients.note: Horse chestnut triterpene saponin used for venotonic/anti-inflammatory effect; saponins are irritants/hemolytic and escin esters are cytotoxic, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: Ethanolamines (MEA/DEA/TEA)
ingredients.note: Can form carcinogenic nitrosamines if combined with nitrosating agents. Triethanolamine alone at low cosmetic levels is widely used and lower-concern; precautionary because the DEA-compounds are the worse actors and labels rarely distinguish risk.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Methacrylate
ingredients.note: FDA-accepted MMA replacement monomer in acrylic nails; a methacrylate contact sensitizer and respiratory/eye irritant requiring ventilation.

ingredients.name: Ethyl Vanillin
ingredients.note: Synthetic vanilla-scented phenolic fragrance ingredient; vanillin-class compounds are EU-flagged fragrance allergens (Reg 2023/1545) with reported contact urticaria.

ingredients.name: Ethylene Brassylate
ingredients.note: Synthetic macrocyclic musk fragrance (CAS 105-95-3). EU has flagged it as a potential skin sensitizer, so treated as a Tier 2 precautionary fragrance.

ingredients.name: Ethylparaben
ingredients.note: Paraben preservative; endocrine-disruptor class.

ingredients.name: Eucalyptus Oil
ingredients.note: Irritant and occasional contact allergen, especially undiluted; 1,8-cineole is the main reactive constituent.

ingredients.name: Eugenia Caryophyllus (Clove) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Clove flower extract rich in eugenol, an EU-declarable fragrance allergen and moderate skin sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Eugenia Caryophyllus (Clove) Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Clove leaf essential oil consisting chiefly of eugenol, a recognized fragrance allergen/sensitizer and irritant. Safe only in low diluted concentrations; patch testing advised.

ingredients.name: Eugenol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a clove scent, found in clove, cinnamon and basil oils. A well-known contact sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Evernia Furfuracea Extract
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen; treemoss is a strong fragrance sensitizer closely related to oakmoss and similarly IFRA-restricted. Of concern for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Evernia Prunastri Extract
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen; oakmoss is one of the most potent and frequent fragrance contact allergens, hence heavily IFRA-restricted. A notable concern for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Farnesol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen and floral-note constituent also used as a deodorant agent. A contact sensitizer relevant to fragrance-allergic skin.

ingredients.name: FD&C Blue No. 1
ingredients.note: Synthetic triarylmethane (coal-tar-derived) dye approved as a color additive. Carries sensitizer and coal-tar-origin concerns; not approved for the eye area by FDA.

ingredients.name: FD&C Green No. 3
ingredients.note: Synthetic triarylmethane coal-tar-derived dye approved as a color additive. Sensitizer/coal-tar-origin concerns; not permitted in the eye area.

ingredients.name: FD&C Red No. 3
ingredients.note: Iodinated xanthene dye with possible-carcinogen and thyroid concerns; its FDA food/ingested authorization has been revoked, and external cosmetic use carries caution. Not for the eye area.

ingredients.name: FD&C Red No. 4
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo dye now restricted to external cosmetics and not permitted in foods or near the eyes due to toxicity findings. Coal-tar/azo concerns warrant caution.

ingredients.name: FD&C Red No. 40
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo dye derived from petroleum. Approved as a color additive but raises sensitization and azo-dye/coal-tar-origin concerns; restricted from eye-area use by FDA.

ingredients.name: FD&C Yellow No. 5
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo (pyrazolone) dye and a recognized sensitizer that can trigger reactions in sensitive individuals. Approved externally but not for use in the eye area.

ingredients.name: FD&C Yellow No. 6
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo dye used as a cosmetic colorant. Coal-tar/azo-dye origin with sensitization concerns; FDA prohibits use in the eye area.

ingredients.name: Flavor/Aroma
ingredients.note: Undisclosed flavoring/aroma blend; composition is proprietary and may include fragrance allergens or sensitizers, so treated as a precautionary sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Formaldehyde
ingredients.note: IARC Group 1 carcinogen. Rare as a direct ingredient now; more often released by the 'releaser' preservatives below.

ingredients.name: Fragrance
ingredients.note: Legal catch-all that can hide undisclosed mixtures including phthalates and dozens of allergens. Precautionary: the word itself isn't proof of harm, but it's the #1 hiding spot — prefer 'fragrance-free' for daily leave-on products.

ingredients.name: Galaxolide
ingredients.note: Polycyclic synthetic musk that is persistent and bioaccumulative with possible endocrine-disrupting activity, under environmental scrutiny in the EU. Skin-sensitization risk is low but bioaccumulation drives the precautionary rating.

ingredients.name: Gaultheria Procumbens (Wintergreen) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Wintergreen extract rich in methyl salicylate. Documented irritation/sensitization and allergic contact dermatitis risk, especially for salicylate-sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Geraniol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a sweet rose odor found in rose and citronella oils. A moderate sensitizer, more so when oxidized.

ingredients.name: Geranium Oil
ingredients.note: Contains geraniol, citronellol and irritant p-cymene; an established fragrance contact allergen.

ingredients.name: Ginkgo Biloba Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Antioxidant botanical extract; CIR concluded it is safe only when formulated to be non-sensitizing, flagging ginkgolic acid as a sensitization concern.

ingredients.name: Glutaral
ingredients.note: Strong aldehyde biocide and potent contact sensitizer/irritant. EU restricts cosmetic use to 0.1% and bans it in aerosol/spray products.

ingredients.name: Glutathione (Injectable/IV Whitening)
ingredients.note: No glutathione product is FDA-approved for skin whitening; injectable use is linked to anaphylaxis, hepatotoxicity, kidney injury, and endotoxin contamination, and has drawn federal injunctions. Avoid injectable/IV whitening preparations.

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Protein
ingredients.note: Soy-derived conditioning protein; CIR reviews plant proteins as safe, but soy is a recognized food allergen so residual protein carries a sensitization concern.

ingredients.name: Glycine Soja (Soybean) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Soybean seed extract rich in antioxidant isoflavones; contains phytoestrogens (genistein/daidzein) and is a potential soy allergen, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Grapefruit Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed peel oil contains furocoumarins making it phototoxic and limonene that can sensitize on air exposure.

ingredients.name: Guanidine Carbonate
ingredients.note: Mixed with calcium hydroxide to form guanidine hydroxide for no-lye relaxers; alkaline and irritating, causing scalp burns with overuse.

ingredients.name: Hamamelis Virginiana (Witch Hazel) Water
ingredients.note: Witch hazel distillate (hydrosol). Astringent, mildly soothing; commercial forms may contain alcohol which can be drying on sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Hamamelis Virginiana Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Witch hazel leaf water (hydrosol). Astringent botanical; gentler than alcohol-containing witch hazel but still mildly toning.

ingredients.name: Hexyl Cinnamal
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a jasmine-like odor widely used in florals. A relatively weak listed sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Homosalate
ingredients.note: Chemical UV filter flagged for endocrine activity; under tightened concentration review in the EU.

ingredients.name: Hydrogen Peroxide
ingredients.note: Oxidizing developer in hair color and bleach; skin/eye irritant and corrosive at higher strengths, with SCCS limits on cosmetic concentration.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Sweet Almond Protein
ingredients.note: Almond-derived conditioning protein; sweet almond is a tree-nut food allergen capable of type-1 reactions, so residual protein poses a sensitization concern despite favorable CIR plant-protein review.

ingredients.name: Hydroxycitronellal
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a sweet lily-of-the-valley scent. A well-documented contact sensitizer, hence IFRA-restricted.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyethyl Methacrylate
ingredients.note: Common gel/acrylic nail monomer and leading methacrylate allergen; acrylates were ACDS Allergen of the Year 2012, with rising allergy from uncured gels.

ingredients.name: Idebenone
ingredients.note: Synthetic ubiquinone-like antioxidant for anti-aging; effective but with documented cases of allergic contact dermatitis. Patch testing advised for sensitive users.

ingredients.name: Illicium Verum (Anise) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Star anise (trans-anethole-rich) extract with antioxidant/antimicrobial activity. Fragrant aromatic constituents can cause skin sensitization and photosensitivity.

ingredients.name: Imidazolidinyl urea
ingredients.note: Formaldehyde releaser; sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Iodopropynyl Butylcarbamate
ingredients.note: Iodine-releasing preservative and contact allergen with restricted use; EU limits concentration and bans it in lip and oral-care products and in products for children under 3 (except bath/shower and shampoo).

ingredients.name: Iris Florentina Root Extract
ingredients.note: Orris (Iridaceae) root extract carrying fragrant irone compounds; possible skin sensitizer in sensitive individuals, so patch testing is advised. Tier raised for caution.

sources.label: PubChem: Iris florentina root extract compound record
PMC: Dual Modulatory Effects of Phytochemicals from Iris germanica var. florentina Rhizome Extract on Melanogenesis
PubMed: Allergic contact dermatitis to Iris germanica root in a natural cosmetic

ingredients.name: Isobutylparaben
ingredients.note: Banned in cosmetics in the EU; treat as avoid.

ingredients.name: Isoeugenol
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen and one of the more potent fragrance sensitizers, hence IFRA-restricted. Of concern chiefly for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Isopropylparaben
ingredients.note: Banned in cosmetics in the EU; treat as avoid.

ingredients.name: Jasmine Absolute
ingredients.note: Solvent-extracted floral absolute containing benzyl benzoate and benzyl acetate; a known fragrance allergen.

ingredients.name: Jasminum Grandiflorum (Jasmine) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Fragrant jasmine flower extract classified as a declarable fragrance allergen under EU 2023/1545; contains linalool/benzyl benzoate sensitizers.

ingredients.name: Jasminum Officinale (Jasmine) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Fragrant jasmine flower extract; an EU-regulated/declarable fragrance allergen that can irritate or sensitize sensitive skin. Used at low levels with patch-test advisory.

ingredients.name: Jasminum Officinale (Jasmine) Flower/Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Jasmine flower/leaf extract; a fragrance allergen under EU 1223/2009 containing benzyl benzoate, benzyl alcohol, and linalool.

ingredients.name: Jasminum Officinale (Jasmine) Oil
ingredients.note: Jasmine essential oil/absolute is a recognized fragrance sensitizer used in patch-test panels; can cause contact allergy in susceptible individuals.

sources.label: PubChem: Oils, jasmine, Jasminum sambac compound record
PMC: Antibacterial Potential Assessment of Jasmine Essential Oil Against E. coli

ingredients.name: Juniperus Virginiana Oil
ingredients.note: Virginian cedarwood essential oil used for fragrance. A recognized contact sensitizer that can cause allergic contact dermatitis; recommended below ~3%.

ingredients.name: Kojic acid
ingredients.note: Brightening agent; common contact sensitizer with some EU concentration limits. Precautionary.

ingredients.name: Kojic Acid Dipalmitate (Adulterant Context)
ingredients.note: A lightening agent of debated stability and a known sensitizer; the EU SCCS restricts kojic acid to 1% and flags sensitization, so high-dose 'whitening' formulas warrant caution.

ingredients.name: Lanolin
ingredients.note: Effective occlusive emollient but a recognized contact sensitizer, especially on damaged or inflamed skin; patch-test if you have sensitive or eczema-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Laureth-4
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated ("-eth-") emulsifier; the surfactant is low concern but ethoxylation can leave trace 1,4-dioxane, an NTP anticipated human carcinogen. The contaminant, not the surfactant, drives the precautionary tier.

ingredients.name: Laureth-7
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated ("-eth-") nonionic surfactant; low-hazard itself but may contain trace 1,4-dioxane unless stripped during manufacture. Precautionary for the contaminant, not the surfactant.

ingredients.name: Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Lavender flower extract used as soothing skin-conditioning agent; gentler than the oil but its aromatic constituents can still cause sensitization in reactive skin.

sources.label: PMC: Safety and efficacy of a feed additive consisting of a tincture from flowers of Lavandula angustifolia (EFSA assessment)
PMC: Phytochemical profiling and in vitro biological activity evaluation of Lavandula angustifolia flower essential oil
PMC: Antimicrobial Properties and Bioactive Compounds of Lavandula angustifolia Mill. Cultivated in Southern Poland

ingredients.name: Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Flower Water
ingredients.note: Lavender flower hydrosol. Soothing aromatic water; far gentler than lavender essential oil but can still carry trace fragrance allergens.

sources.label: PMC: Safety and efficacy of a feed additive consisting of a tincture from flowers of Lavandula angustifolia (EFSA assessment)
PMC: Phytochemical profiling and in vitro biological activity evaluation of Lavandula angustifolia flower essential oil
PMC: Antimicrobial Properties and Bioactive Compounds of Lavandula angustifolia Mill. Cultivated in Southern Poland

ingredients.name: Lavandula Angustifolia (Lavender) Oil
ingredients.note: Lavender essential oil (linalool/linalyl acetate chemotype); a recognized contact allergen on the 2023 EU fragrance-allergen list and dermal/respiratory sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Lavender Oil
ingredients.note: Well-documented contact sensitizer, largely via linalool and linalyl acetate that form allergenic oxidation products on air exposure.

ingredients.name: Lemongrass Oil
ingredients.note: Citral-dominant oil; citral is irritant and sensitizing, so use levels are IFRA-restricted.

ingredients.name: Lime Oil
ingredients.note: Expressed lime oil is among the more phototoxic citrus oils (high furocoumarins); steam-distilled lime is far less phototoxic.

ingredients.name: Limonene
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen and citrus-scented terpene found in most citrus oils. Oxidizes on air exposure into a markedly more potent sensitizer; concern is largely for reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Linalool
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen found in lavender, bergamot and many florals; oxidized forms can sensitize. Concern is largely limited to reactive skin.

ingredients.name: m-Aminophenol
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye coupler and contact sensitizer; SCCS-evaluated with maximum-use concentration limits in hair colorants.

ingredients.name: Magnolia Champaca Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Fragrant champaca flower essential oil rich in linalool; IFRA-restricted (~0.3%) and a potential sensitizer/allergen.

ingredients.name: Magnolia Officinalis Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Magnolia bark extract (magnolol/honokiol) used as an antioxidant/anti-inflammatory botanical, but identified as a contact allergen in anti-ageing cosmetics. Tier 2 precautionary as a sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Extract
ingredients.note: Tea tree-derived material whose terpene constituents (terpinolene, terpinenes, limonene, plus oxidation products like ascaridole) are documented sensitizers. CIR found tea-tree ingredients safe only when oxidation is minimized.

ingredients.name: Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Tea tree-derived extract. Oxidized/aged product is a recognized contact sensitizer (ascaridole, terpinene, limonene); SCCP advises minimizing oxidation.

ingredients.name: Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Tea tree essential oil; a known contact sensitizer especially when oxidized (ascaridole, 1,8-cineole, terpenes). CIR/SCCS deem it safe only at limited non-sensitizing concentrations.

ingredients.name: Melaleuca Alternifolia (Tea Tree) Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Tea tree leaf hydrosol. Mild antimicrobial botanical water; much gentler than concentrated tea tree essential oil.

ingredients.name: Mentha Arvensis Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Cornmint essential oil with very high menthol content; contains naturally-occurring fragrance allergens (limonene, linalool) and can irritate or sensitize at higher levels. Restricted to low use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Mentha Piperita (Peppermint) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Peppermint leaf extract used as a refreshing skin conditioner; CIR deems it safe only when formulated to be non-sensitizing, with menthol enhancing penetration and isolated sensitization reports. Flagged precautionary as a potential sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Meradimate
ingredients.note: Weak UVA anthranilate filter that the FDA has not found GRASE and that the EU SCCS recommended against using over safety data gaps. Considered an older, poorly characterized filter.

ingredients.name: Methenamine
ingredients.note: Slow formaldehyde releaser and contact allergen; EU-permitted up to 0.15% as a preservative. Concern stems from formaldehyde liberation and sensitization.

ingredients.name: Methyl 2-Octynoate
ingredients.note: EU-26 declarable allergen with a green violet-leaf odor; a potent sensitizer that is strongly IFRA-restricted. Relevant mainly for fragrance-reactive skin.

ingredients.name: Methylchloroisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Potent contact sensitizer, almost always used as the MCI/MI blend (Kathon CG). EU permits the 3:1 MCI/MI mixture only in rinse-off products at max 15 ppm.

ingredients.name: Methyleugenol
ingredients.note: Fragrance constituent found in essential oils that is both a contact sensitizer and a genotoxic carcinogen of regulatory concern, hence strictly IFRA-limited. Uncertain/mixed profile warrants a precautionary stance.

ingredients.name: Methylisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Major contact allergen named Contact Allergen of the Year (2013). Banned in EU leave-on cosmetics and capped at 15 ppm in rinse-off products.

ingredients.name: Methylparaben
ingredients.note: Weak estrogen-mimicking preservative. Usually labeled, so easy to avoid. Risk is cumulative; reversible on discontinuation.

ingredients.name: Michelia Alba Flower Oil
ingredients.note: White champaca essential oil very high in linalool (plus geraniol); oxidized linalool is a known skin sensitizer and an EU-listed fragrance allergen.

ingredients.name: Musk Ketone
ingredients.note: Nitromusk that is persistent and bioaccumulative; permitted only under tight EU concentration limits and largely phased out. Rated precautionary due to bioaccumulation and toxicity concerns.

ingredients.name: Musk Xylene
ingredients.note: Nitromusk that is persistent, bioaccumulative and an SVHC of very high concern; banned/restricted in EU cosmetics, so it should not appear in compliant products. Precautionary flag is false because it is effectively prohibited.

ingredients.name: Myristica Fragrans (Nutmeg) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Spice essential oil containing myristicin, which is toxic at high doses; potential for skin sensitivity at higher concentrations. Treated cautiously.

ingredients.name: Nickel
ingredients.note: The most common contact allergen worldwide, present as a trace contaminant in pigments and eye cosmetics; even low levels can trigger allergic contact dermatitis in sensitized users.

ingredients.name: Nonapeptide-1
ingredients.note: Synthetic biomimetic peptide acting as an alpha-MSH antagonist at MC1R to inhibit melanogenesis (skin lightening). Receptor-active depigmenting mechanism warrants precaution.

sources.label: PubMed: Enhanced skin benefits of EGCG loaded in nonapeptide-1-conjugated mesoporous silica nanoparticles to reverse skin photoaging
PMC: Alpha-Melanocyte Stimulating Hormone: An Emerging Anti-Inflammatory Antimicrobial Peptide

ingredients.name: o-Aminophenol
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye coupler and skin sensitizer; SCCS-restricted in permanent hair-dye formulations.

ingredients.name: Ocimum Basilicum (Basil) Flower/Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Basil-derived material can contain estragole/methyl chavicol, a flagged sensitizer with toxicity concerns at high exposure. Treated cautiously as a potential sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Ocimum Basilicum (Basil) Oil
ingredients.note: Basil essential oil contains estragole (methyl chavicol), a genotoxic carcinogen flag, plus linalool/eugenol sensitizers; IFRA restricts high-estragole chemotypes. Treated as a precautionary fragrance allergen.

ingredients.name: Octinoxate
ingredients.note: UV filter shown to have estrogenic / weak anti-androgenic activity. Regulators still permit it up to ~10%, but for daily leave-on use a mineral filter avoids the question.

ingredients.name: Octocrylene
ingredients.note: Common photostabilizer and UVB/short-UVA filter that can degrade over time into benzophenone, a suspected endocrine disruptor and carcinogen. Detectable in plasma above FDA thresholds and a known contact/photo allergen.

ingredients.name: Octylisothiazolinone
ingredients.note: Isothiazolinone contact allergen used as an in-can/material preservative. Not an approved cosmetic preservative in the EU and a recognized sensitization concern.

ingredients.name: Oligopeptide-1
ingredients.note: Recombinant human EGF used cosmetically to promote cell renewal. Treated cautiously because long-term safety and skin penetration of a proliferation-signaling growth factor are not well established.

ingredients.name: Origanum Majorana Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Marjoram leaf material can carry fragrance allergens (linalool, limonene, geraniol) and is reported as a dermal sensitizer/irritant. Treated as a precautionary sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Origanum Vulgare Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Oregano leaf extract rich in phenols carvacrol and thymol, with antimicrobial/antioxidant activity but known potential to irritate or sensitize skin, especially when concentrated. Treated as a precautionary sensitizer botanical.

ingredients.name: Oxybenzone
ingredients.note: Chemical sunscreen filter with endocrine activity in lab/animal studies and notable skin-absorption. Mineral filters (zinc oxide, titanium dioxide) are the clean swap.

ingredients.name: p-Aminophenol
ingredients.note: Oxidative hair-dye intermediate and known skin sensitizer; SCCS-restricted, with mutagenicity concerns at high exposure.

ingredients.name: p-Phenylenediamine
ingredients.note: Permanent hair-dye agent; potent allergen capable of severe reactions. Mainly hair, not face.

ingredients.name: Padimate O
ingredients.note: PABA-derivative UVB filter that has fallen out of favor over allergy, photo-allergenicity and potential to form nitrosamines or generate free radicals. Largely replaced by newer filters.

ingredients.name: Papain
ingredients.note: Proteolytic enzyme used for exfoliation. A recognized skin and respiratory sensitizer; can induce allergic sensitization through skin even when enzymatically inactivated.

ingredients.name: PEG-100 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated stearate emulsifier (usually paired with glyceryl stearate). Safe as used, but PEG/ethoxylated ingredients can carry trace 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide residues, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: PEG-12 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Water-dispersible silicone surfactant; the linear silicone backbone is low concern, but the PEG (ethoxylated) portion can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless stripped. Precautionary for the byproduct contaminant.

ingredients.name: PEG-40 Hydrogenated Castor Oil
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated castor oil used to solubilize fragrances and oils. Effective and broadly safe, but as an ethoxylate it may carry trace 1,4-dioxane.

ingredients.name: PEG-40 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated emulsifier/solubilizer. Generally safe, but ethoxylation can leave trace 1,4-dioxane/ethylene oxide, warranting precaution.

ingredients.name: PEG-7 Glyceryl Cocoate
ingredients.note: Mild emollient surfactant; the PEG ester is well tolerated, but as an ethoxylated ingredient it may contain trace 1,4-dioxane (anticipated human carcinogen) unless stripped. Precautionary for the contaminant only.

ingredients.name: PEG-8
ingredients.note: Low-molecular-weight humectant/solvent that is itself low-hazard, but as an ethoxylated material it may carry trace 1,4-dioxane (an anticipated human carcinogen) unless purified. Tiered precautionary for the potential contaminant, not the PEG.

ingredients.name: PEG-8 Stearate
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated emulsifier that is itself low concern but can carry trace 1,4-dioxane from the ethoxylation process. The carcinogenic byproduct, removable by purification, is the reason for the precautionary tier.

ingredients.name: PEG-80 Sorbitan Laurate
ingredients.note: Mild PEG-based nonionic surfactant common in gentle and baby cleansers; being ethoxylated it can carry trace 1,4-dioxane unless purified. The surfactant itself is low-irritation.

ingredients.name: Pelargonium Graveolens Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Geranium essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. EFSA classifies it a skin and respiratory sensitizer; contains geraniol/citronellol EU fragrance allergens.

ingredients.name: Peppermint Oil
ingredients.note: Can cause contact allergy and perioral/hairline dermatitis; menthol and limonene are irritant and sensitizing in susceptible users.

ingredients.name: Peru Balsam
ingredients.note: Classic marker allergen on patch testing; rich in cinnamates and benzoates, so it is largely restricted in modern leave-on cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Phenethyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Aromatic alcohol used as preservative-booster, solvent and rose-scented fragrance. CIR safe to ~1%, but it is a fragrance ingredient that can sensitize fragrance-allergic individuals.

sources.label: CIR: Phenethyl alcohol safety assessment page
PubMed: RIFM fragrance ingredient safety assessment, phenethyl alcohol

ingredients.name: Piper Methysticum Leaf/Root/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Kava plant extract used as a skin conditioner; CIR found the data insufficient to support safety and topical use has been linked to skin sensitization and dermopathy. Flagged precautionary for uncertain/mixed evidence.

ingredients.name: Pogostemon Cablin Oil
ingredients.note: Patchouli essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. Considered an irritant and respiratory sensitizer; can irritate skin above recommended 0.5-2%.

ingredients.name: Pollen Extract
ingredients.note: Bee-collected pollen used as an antioxidant/soothing extract. A recognized cause of hypersensitivity reactions in bee/pollen-allergic individuals.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylamide
ingredients.note: Acrylamide-based polymer used as a thickener/stabilizer. The polymer is inert, but residual acrylamide monomer is a recognized neurotoxin/probable carcinogen, so SCCS limits residuals and it is flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Polyethylene
ingredients.note: Solid polyethylene microbeads are an environmental microplastic concern and are being phased out under EU REACH restrictions. Restricted rather than a direct skin-safety hazard.

ingredients.name: Potassium Persulfate
ingredients.note: Persulfate oxidizer in lightening powders; a respiratory sensitizer linked to asthma and a known contact allergen.

ingredients.name: Propolis
ingredients.note: Resinous bee-collected plant exudate and a well-known contact allergen, originally in beekeepers; increasingly seen with cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Propyl Gallate
ingredients.note: Gallate-ester antioxidant used to prevent rancidity of oils. Documented skin sensitizer; CIR limits it to 0.1% in cosmetics, hence cautionary.

ingredients.name: Propylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Common humectant and solvent; a weak but recognized contact allergen (ACDS Allergen of the Year 2018), so worth reducing for sensitive or eczema-prone skin. Safe for most users at typical levels.

ingredients.name: Propylparaben
ingredients.note: Endocrine-disruptor concern; among the more estrogenic parabens.

ingredients.name: Pueraria Lobata Root Extract
ingredients.note: Kudzu root extract rich in isoflavones (puerarin, daidzein) with documented phytoestrogenic activity, raising an endocrine concern. Legume-related allergy possible.

ingredients.name: Pulsatilla Koreana Extract
ingredients.note: Korean pasque flower extract used mainly for antimicrobial/natural-preservative activity (often paired with Usnea barbata and Zanthoxylum). Documented cases of irritant contact dermatitis warrant caution.

sources.label: PMC: Pulsatilla koreana Nakai Extract Attenuates Atopic Dermatitis-like Symptoms by Regulating Skin Barrier Factors and Inhibiting the JAK/STAT Pathway
PubMed: Anti-inflammatory effects of methanol extract from Pulsatilla koreana in lipopolysaccharide-exposed rats
PubChem: Cosmetic composition with whitening effect comprising extract of pulsatilla radix (patent)

ingredients.name: Quaternium-15
ingredients.note: Formaldehyde-releasing preservative and a frequent contact-allergy trigger.

ingredients.name: Red 30 Lake (CI 73360)
ingredients.note: Insoluble thioindigoid lake pigment, FDA-approved for cosmetics including lips but not for the eye area; subject to FDA specifications. Possible dye sensitivity.

ingredients.name: Red 33 (CI 17200)
ingredients.note: Water-soluble azo dye, FDA color additive permitted in cosmetics with limits (lip products up to 3% color), not for eye area. Some individuals sensitive to azo dyes.

ingredients.name: Red 7 (CI 15850)
ingredients.note: Synthetic azo lake pigment (lithol rubine) FDA-certified for cosmetics including lips, but not permitted in eye-area products. Batch certification required; possible dye sensitivity.

sources.label: PubChem: D&C Red No. 6 / Lithol Rubine compound record (CID 135487652)
PubMed: Identification and quantification of decarboxylated analogue of Pigments Red 57 and 57:1 in D&C Red No. 6, D&C Red No. 7, and their lakes

ingredients.name: Resorcinol
ingredients.note: Hair-dye ingredient with thyroid-disruption and skin-sensitization concerns.

ingredients.name: Retinal
ingredients.note: Retinaldehyde, the aldehyde form of vitamin A and a potent retinoid one step from retinoic acid, used as an anti-aging/anti-acne active. Can cause irritation and photosensitivity and is a vitamin-A-derivative endocrine/retinoid concern, so treated as precautionary.

ingredients.name: Retinyl Linoleate
ingredients.note: Retinyl ester (vitamin A) retinoid; SCCS restricts vitamin A esters and advises caution in leave-on products and during pregnancy. Treated as endocrine/teratogen-adjacent concern.

ingredients.name: Retinyl palmitate
ingredients.note: Mild cosmetic retinoid. Precautionary Tier 2 only because of debated photo-co-carcinogenicity data and pregnancy caution around high vitamin-A; routine low-level cosmetic use is generally considered fine.

ingredients.name: Rosa Damascena Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Damask rose essential oil used as fragrance/skin-conditioner. Contains EU fragrance allergens citronellol, geraniol, linalool and farnesol; can sensitize/irritate.

ingredients.name: Rosa Damascena Flower Water
ingredients.note: Damask rose hydrosol (rose water). Soothing, hydrating aromatic water; gentle, though it can carry trace rose-fragrance allergens.

ingredients.name: Rose Extract
ingredients.note: Rose flower extract used for fragrance and conditioning; naturally contains fragrance allergens (citronellol, geraniol, eugenol, linalool) that can sensitize, especially when oxidized.

ingredients.name: Rosemary Oil
ingredients.note: Aromatic oil containing camphor, limonene and pinenes that can provoke contact allergy; distinct from low-allergen rosemary leaf extract used as antioxidant.

ingredients.name: Rosmarinus Officinalis (Rosemary) Leaf Oil
ingredients.note: Rosemary essential oil contains monoterpenes and linalool that can cause contact irritation/sensitization in susceptible individuals. Treated as a potential sensitizer (Tier 2, precautionary).

ingredients.name: Royal Jelly Extract
ingredients.note: Bee-derived skin-conditioning extract; major royal jelly proteins (MRJP1/MRJP2) are documented allergens that can trigger reactions in bee-sensitive users.

sources.label: PMC: Bee Products in Dermatology and Skin Care
PMC: Royal Jelly Protects against Epidermal Stress through Upregulation of NQO1 Expression
PMC: Effect of Protease-Treated Royal Jelly Extract on Facial Wrinkles: A Placebo-Controlled, Double-Blind, Parallel-Group Study

ingredients.name: Salvia Sclarea (Clary) Oil
ingredients.note: Clary sage essential oil rich in linalool and linalyl acetate (linalool is an established fragrance allergen). Used as a natural fragrance/skin tonic; irritation/allergy is the chief concern.

ingredients.name: Sandalwood Oil
ingredients.note: A recognized fragrance contact allergen; can cause allergic contact dermatitis and photoallergy in sensitized individuals.

ingredients.name: Saponins
ingredients.note: Plant glycosides used as natural surfactants/cleansers; saponins are gastrointestinal/mucosal irritants and can be hemolytic, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-1
ingredients.note: Recombinant human basic Fibroblast Growth Factor (bFGF/FGF2) analog. Bioactive growth factor with debated topical safety; precautionary.

sources.label: PubMed: Topical application of sh-oligopeptide-1 and clinical trials with cosmetic preparations: risk or fraud?
PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-22
ingredients.note: Recombinant human Transforming Growth Factor beta-1 (TGFbeta-1) analog. Bioactive growth factor whose topical safety is debated; precautionary.

sources.label: PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review (includes TGF-beta polypeptides)

ingredients.name: sh-Polypeptide-3
ingredients.note: Recombinant human Keratinocyte Growth Factor (KGF) analog. Bioactive growth factor with debated topical safety; precautionary.

sources.label: PMC: Skin rejuvenation using cosmetic products containing growth factors, cytokines, and matrikines: a review

ingredients.name: Silica
ingredients.note: Amorphous (cosmetic) silica is inert and safe in creams and pressed products. Loose powders pose an inhalation caution; crystalline silica is the hazardous form and should not be present in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Sodium hydroxymethylglycinate
ingredients.note: Often marketed as a 'natural' preservative but releases formaldehyde. Precautionary Tier 2 due to the green-washing framing.

ingredients.name: Sodium Laureth Sulfate
ingredients.note: Milder than SLS but ethoxylated, so it can carry trace 1,4-dioxane (a probable carcinogen) unless vacuum-stripped during manufacture. The dioxane is a manufacturing contaminant, not an inherent property; reputable suppliers reduce it to negligible levels.

ingredients.name: Sodium Metabisulfite
ingredients.note: Sulfite preservative and antioxidant that can trigger contact allergy and, in sensitive individuals, respiratory or urticarial reactions. EU permits it within Annex V limits (up to 0.2% as free SO2).

ingredients.name: Sodium Persulfate
ingredients.note: Persulfate bleach accelerator; associated with occupational asthma and contact dermatitis among salon workers and clients.

ingredients.name: Sorbitan Sesquioleate
ingredients.note: Common nonionic sorbitan-ester emulsifier, but a documented and emerging contact allergen used in patch-test series. Flagged precautionary for sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Steareth-2
ingredients.note: Low-mole ethoxylated stearyl alcohol used as a W/O co-emulsifier. Ethoxylated process can leave trace 1,4-dioxane; flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Steareth-20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated ("-eth-") emulsifier with the same trace 1,4-dioxane caveat as other ethoxylated surfactants. The PEG chain is well tolerated; the byproduct contaminant is the basis for precaution.

ingredients.name: Steareth-21
ingredients.note: Higher-mole ethoxylated stearyl alcohol emulsifier. Safe in use but ethoxylated, hence trace 1,4-dioxane precaution.

ingredients.name: Sweet Orange Oil
ingredients.note: High-limonene citrus oil; mildly phototoxic and a fragrance allergen, with limonene oxidation products driving sensitization.

ingredients.name: Symphytum Officinale (Comfrey) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Comfrey can contain hepatotoxic/genotoxic pyrrolizidine alkaloids; oral use is FDA-banned and EMA restricts topical use, though dermal absorption of lycopsamine is low. Treated as a sensitizer/toxicant concern.

ingredients.name: Talc
ingredients.note: Talc is mined near asbestos and can be contaminated with it; the FDA still must establish standardized asbestos testing under MoCRA. Creams and pressed products are generally fine, but loose face/body powders carry an inhalation and contamination caution.

ingredients.name: Tea Tree Oil
ingredients.note: Common contact allergen; oxidized oil is markedly more sensitizing, with ascaridole, terpinene and limonene as key haptens. Sensitization is cumulative and reversible.

ingredients.name: Thymus Vulgaris (Thyme) Extract
ingredients.note: Thyme extract whose key constituents (thymol, carvacrol) are recognized skin sensitizers/allergens; restricted to low levels in leave-on products. Treated as a Tier 2 sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Toluene
ingredients.note: Neurotoxic solvent, mostly in nail polish. Inhalation/repeated exposure concern; part of the nail 'toxic trio.'

ingredients.name: Toluene-2,5-Diamine
ingredients.note: Primary oxidative hair-dye intermediate and strong skin sensitizer closely related to PPD; SCCS-restricted with maximum-use limits.

ingredients.name: Tonalide
ingredients.note: Polycyclic synthetic musk that is persistent and bioaccumulative with suspected endocrine-disrupting effects. Flagged precautionary primarily for environmental and bioaccumulation concerns rather than acute skin risk.

ingredients.name: Tosylamide/Formaldehyde Resin
ingredients.note: Nail-lacquer film-former and well-known cause of allergic contact dermatitis (including ectopic eyelid/face) due to residual formaldehyde reactivity.

ingredients.name: Triclocarban
ingredients.note: Antibacterial relative of triclosan; same endocrine-disruptor concern.

ingredients.name: Triclosan
ingredients.note: Antibacterial in some cleansers/soaps; endocrine-disruptor and antibiotic-resistance concerns. Largely removed from US hand soaps but still appears in other products.

ingredients.name: Tussilago Farfara (Coltsfoot) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Coltsfoot is an Asteraceae plant containing pyrrolizidine alkaloids (hepatotoxic) and is a potential Compositae sensitizer; flagged precautionary despite topical cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Usnea Barbata (Lichen) Extract
ingredients.note: Lichen extract containing usnic acid, used as a co-preservative/antimicrobial; noted sensitization potential and usnic acid is hepatotoxic if ingested. Marked precautionary for sensitizer concern.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Vanilla flower botanical extract; vanilla-derived aromatics include vanillin, an EU-flagged fragrance allergen, so treated precautionarily despite generally mild profile.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Extract
