ingredients.note: Vanilla fruit extract is largely non-sensitizing per CIR but contains vanillin, an EU-listed declarable fragrance allergen, so handled precautionarily.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Oil
ingredients.note: Vanilla-derived oil containing vanillin, which the EU now lists as a declarable fragrance allergen (Reg 2023/1545); generally mild but flagged for sensitization potential.

ingredients.name: Vanilla Planifolia Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Vanilla fruit water (aqueous fraction). Mild aromatic botanical; can contribute trace fragrance compounds.

ingredients.name: Vanillin
ingredients.note: Aromatic aldehyde fragrance/flavor agent now individually labelled as a fragrance allergen under EU Regulation 2023/1545; secondary sensitizer linked to vanilla/isoeugenol cross-reactivity.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Vanilla-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Vitex Agnus-Castus Extract
ingredients.note: Chasteberry extract with documented estrogen-receptor-beta-selective phytoconstituents (apigenin) and dopaminergic/hormonal activity; endocrine concern.

ingredients.name: Witch Hazel
ingredients.note: Astringent extract considered safe when non-irritating; alcohol-based distillates and high levels can dry or irritate sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Yellow 5 (CI 19140)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic azo dye that causes the most allergic/intolerance reactions of the azo dyes, especially in aspirin-sensitive or asthmatic individuals.

sources.label: PubChem: FD&C Yellow No. 5 compound record (CID 16013)
PMC: Toxicological Effects of Tartrazine Exposure: A Review of In Vitro and Animal Studies with Human Health Implications
FDA: How Safe are Color Additives?

ingredients.name: Yellow 6 (CI 15985)
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived azo dye (Sunset Yellow), FDA-certified color additive. Animal studies show no carcinogenicity, but it can trigger allergic-type reactions in sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Ylang Ylang Oil
ingredients.note: Floral oil rich in linalool, benzyl benzoate and isoeugenol; one of the higher-risk essential oils for allergic contact dermatitis.

ingredients.name: Zanthoxylum Piperitum Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Japanese pepper (sansho) extract containing tingling sanshool alkylamides plus limonene/terpene fragrance components; can sensitize/irritate, conservative dosing advised.

ingredients.name: Zinc Pyrithione
ingredients.note: Anti-dandruff antimicrobial reclassified as reprotoxic (CMR) in the EU, leading to its prohibition as a cosmetic preservative as of 2022. Can also irritate skin and eyes.

ingredients.name: Zingiber Officinale (Ginger) Root Oil
ingredients.note: Steam-distilled ginger essential oil; can cause skin irritation or sensitization in susceptible individuals, patch testing advised.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Zingiber officinale (Ginger)-Derived Ingredients (main report)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Zingiber officinale (Ginger)-Derived Ingredients (supporting literature review)
PMC: Safety and efficacy of essential oil, oleoresin and tincture from Zingiber officinale Roscoe as sensory additives in feed

ingredients.name: 1,2-Hexanediol
ingredients.note: Multifunctional glycol acting as solvent, humectant and preservative-booster; CIR concluded 1,2-glycols safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: 2,3-Butanediol
ingredients.note: Small diol functioning as humectant, solvent and skin conditioner; standard cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety evaluation of some humectants and moisturizers used in cosmetic formulations

ingredients.name: 3-O-Ethyl ascorbic acid
ingredients.note: Stable vitamin C derivative; antioxidant/brightening, safe.

ingredients.name: 4-Terpineol
ingredients.note: Monoterpene alcohol (main active of tea tree oil) used as a fragrance/antimicrobial agent. RIFM evaluated it as safe at current fragrance levels with markedly lower sensitization risk than whole tea tree oil.

ingredients.name: 7-Dehydrocholesterol
ingredients.note: The cholesterol-pathway precursor of vitamin D3, used as a skin-conditioning/barrier-support lipid. Used at very low levels and considered a benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Acacia Decurrens Flower Wax
ingredients.note: Floral plant wax from Acacia decurrens used as an emollient, structuring and film-forming agent. Standard botanical wax with low irritation potential.

sources.label: PubChem: Acacia decurrens flower compound record
CIR: Safety Assessment of Acacia Senegal Gum and related Acacia-derived ingredients

ingredients.name: Acacia Senegal Gum
ingredients.note: Plant polysaccharide gum used as film-former, binder and emulsion stabilizer; CIR concluded safe with low toxicity.

ingredients.name: Acacia Seyal Gum
ingredients.note: Acacia tree polysaccharide gum used as a film-former, thickener and texture agent. Benign; closely related to CIR-reviewed acacia gums.

ingredients.name: Acer Saccharum (Sugar Maple) Extract
ingredients.note: Sugar maple extract (a natural AHA source) used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Acer Saccharum Extract
ingredients.note: Sugar maple sap extract containing mild naturally-occurring AHAs (malic, tartaric) and polyphenols, used for gentle skin conditioning/antioxidant benefit. Weak exfoliant; benign cosmetic botanical.

sources.label: PubMed: Nutrients, Antioxidant Capacity and Safety of Hot Water Extract from Sugar Maple (Acer saccharum) and Red Maple Bark
PMC: Chemical Compositional, Biological, and Safety Studies of a Novel Maple Syrup Derived Extract for Nutraceutical Applications
PubMed: Phenolic glycosides from sugar maple (Acer saccharum) bark

ingredients.name: Acetone
ingredients.note: Standard nail-polish remover solvent of low systemic toxicity; drying and defatting to skin/nails, an inhalation irritant, and highly flammable.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Dipeptide-1 Cetyl Ester
ingredients.note: Synthetic lipo-dipeptide signal peptide marketed for firming and skin-calming; no significant safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Glucosamine
ingredients.note: Amino sugar used as a skin-conditioning agent supporting hydration and exfoliation. CIR concluded glucosamine ingredients are safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Hexapeptide-1
ingredients.note: Synthetic alpha-MSH biomimetic peptide that stimulates melanocortin-1 receptor for skin/hair pigmentation. Used at low levels; low irritation/sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Acetyl hexapeptide-8
ingredients.note: Cosmetic peptide; safe.

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-5
ingredients.note: Synthetic anti-edema tetrapeptide used in eye products to reduce puffiness and dark circles. Considered safe and non-toxic topically.

sources.label: PMC: Usage of Synthetic Peptides in Cosmetics for Sensitive Skin
PubMed: Protective and Anti-Aging Effects of 5 Cosmeceutical Peptides

ingredients.name: Acetyl Tetrapeptide-9
ingredients.note: Synthetic matrikine peptide (Dermican, Ac-Gln-Asp-Val-His) that mimics lumican to support collagen synthesis. Well-tolerated cosmetic peptide active.

sources.label: PMC: Usage of Synthetic Peptides in Cosmetics for Sensitive Skin
PubMed: Regulatory role of the tetrapeptide AcSDKP in skin and hair physiology

ingredients.name: Acrylates Copolymer
ingredients.note: Fully polymerized acrylic copolymer used as a film former and rheology modifier. Inert as used; only unreacted acrylamide/acrylate monomers are a concern, and finished polymer grades are tightly controlled.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ammonium Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer functioning as a film former, fixative and viscosity/emulsion stabilizer. High-molecular-weight polymer with low toxicity and minimal residual monomer per CIR.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Carbomer-family acrylic copolymer that thickens and helps emulsify oils. Considered safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Dimethicone Copolymer
ingredients.note: Hybrid acrylic-silicone polymer giving water- and transfer-resistant films; inert and generally recognized as safe.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylic copolymer used as a film former in sunscreens, foundations and styling products. Large inert polymer; standard formulation aid.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Ethylhexyl Acrylate/Dimethicone Methacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Silicone-acrylate copolymer used as a film-former and texture/wear agent in color and sun care products. Standard low-concern polymer.

sources.label: PubChem: Acrylic acid/dimethicone methacrylate/ethylhexyl acrylate copolymer compound record
CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Acrylates Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Palmeth-25 Acrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a viscosity/thickening and texture agent. Judged safe by CIR for the acrylate polymer family.

ingredients.name: Acrylates/Polytrimethylsiloxymethacrylate Copolymer
ingredients.note: High-molecular-weight silicone-acrylate film former used in color, sun and hair care. CIR reviewed acrylates copolymers as safe; large inert polymer with little toxicity.

ingredients.name: Actinidia Chinensis (Kiwi) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Kiwi seed oil high in linoleic and alpha-linolenic acids, used as an emollient and antioxidant skin-conditioning oil. Benign.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Baobab fruit extract used as an antioxidant (vitamin C) and skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Pulp Extract
ingredients.note: Baobab fruit pulp extract, rich in vitamin C and polysaccharides, used as an antioxidant and humectant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign.

ingredients.name: Adansonia Digitata Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Baobab seed oil rich in oleic/linoleic/palmitic acids and vitamin E; non-comedogenic emollient.

sources.label: PubChem: Adansonia digitata seed oil compound record
CIR: Adansonia digitata seed oil ingredient page
PMC: Impact of Different Oil Extraction Techniques on the Physicochemical Properties of Adansonia digitata Seed

ingredients.name: Adenosine
ingredients.note: Signaling ingredient for smoothing; safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Aframomum Angustifolium Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Ginger-family (Zingiberaceae) seed extract used as an antioxidant, anti-aging skin-conditioning botanical; reported suitable for sensitive skin. Benign.

ingredients.name: Agar
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide gel from red algae used as a gelling, thickening and stabilizing agent in creams, gels and emulsions.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Plant Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (includes Agar)
CIR: Safety Assessment of Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (final report)

ingredients.name: Agave Tequilana Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Agave leaf extract used as a humectant/skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Alanine
ingredients.note: Amino acid and NMF constituent used as a skin-conditioning humectant. CIR concluded alpha-amino acids are safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Alcaligenes Polysaccharides
ingredients.note: Bacterial-fermentation polysaccharide gum used as a high-capacity humectant, emulsion stabilizer and viscosity agent. CIR microbial-gum assessment supports safety.

ingredients.name: Alchemilla Vulgaris Extract
ingredients.note: Lady's mantle extract, a tannin-rich astringent and antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard, low-hazard plant extract.

sources.label: PMC: Harnessing the Power of Alchemilla: A Natural Solution for Skin Health and Dermatological Disorders
PubChem: Alchemilla vulgaris, extract compound record
PubMed: Protective effect of dietary Alchemilla mollis on UVB-irradiated premature skin aging

ingredients.name: Alcohol Denat.
ingredients.note: Denatured ethanol used as a fast-evaporating solvent; low systemic concern but can be drying and irritating on compromised or sensitive skin at high concentration, and is flammable. Precautionary mainly for its drying/barrier-disrupting potential.

ingredients.name: Algae Extract
ingredients.note: Generic marine algae/seaweed extract used as a skin-conditioning and humectant agent. Generally well-tolerated; possible trace arsenic controlled by GMP.

ingredients.name: Algin
ingredients.note: Brown-seaweed-derived polysaccharide (sodium alginate) used as a thickener/gelling and film-forming agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Alginic Acid
ingredients.note: Brown-algae polysaccharide used as a thickening/gelling and binding agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Allantoin
ingredients.note: Soothing, skin-protectant; safe.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis (Aloe Vera) Leaf Juice
ingredients.note: Aloe vera leaf juice used as a soothing/moisturizing botanical. CIR deems it safe when anthraquinone (aloin) levels stay below 50 ppm.

sources.label: PubChem: Aloe barbadensis extract compound record (CID 5712222)
PMC: Aloe vera: A review of toxicity and adverse clinical effects
PMC: Toxicologic Assessment of a Commercial Decolorized Whole Leaf Aloe Vera Juice

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Aloe flower botanical used for skin conditioning; CIR supports safety when anthraquinone content is limited, and anthraquinones can be phototoxic, so precautionary.

ingredients.name: Aloe Barbadensis Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Aloe vera hydrosol — the watery distillate/juice fraction of aloe leaf. Soothing, hydrating humectant; very widely tolerated.

sources.label: PubChem: Aloe barbadensis extract compound record (CID 5712222)
PMC: Aloe vera: A review of toxicity and adverse clinical effects
PMC: Toxicologic Assessment of a Commercial Decolorized Whole Leaf Aloe Vera Juice

ingredients.name: Aloe Ferox Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Soothing aloe-derived leaf extract used as a skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe for topical use.

ingredients.name: Aloe Vera
ingredients.note: Hydrating, soothing leaf gel that is very well tolerated; rare allergy occurs but it is benign for most users.

ingredients.name: Alpha-Glucan
ingredients.note: Glucose-based polysaccharide used as a humectant, prebiotic and barrier-supporting skin-conditioning agent. Gentle and non-irritating, suitable for sensitive skin.

sources.label: PMC: Prebiotic Oligosaccharides in Skin Health: Benefits, Mechanisms, and Cosmetic Applications
PubMed: Skin health promotion effects of natural beta-glucan derived from cereals and microorganisms

ingredients.name: Alteromonas Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: Marine-bacteria fermentation extract (exopolysaccharide) used for skin conditioning and hydration; considered well tolerated and low risk.

sources.label: Anti-aging cosmetic with Alteromonas ferment extract - PMC clinical study
Marine versus Non-Marine Bacterial Exopolysaccharides and Skincare - PMC
Application of Marine Microbial Natural Products in Cosmetics - PMC

ingredients.name: Althaea Rosea Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Hollyhock flower extract used as a soothing, mucilage-rich skin-conditioning botanical. Standard low-hazard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Alumina
ingredients.note: Inert aluminum oxide used as an abrasive, bulking and opacifying agent and pigment substrate. Insoluble mineral considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Dimyristate
ingredients.note: Aluminum salt of myristic acid used as a thickener, gellant and viscosity/texture agent (often to gel oils). Inert metallic soap regarded as safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Inert substrate for lake colorants and opacifying/pigment-coating agent; CIR found alumina/aluminum hydroxide safe with negligible systemic aluminum.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Starch Octenylsuccinate
ingredients.note: Modified starch powder used to absorb oil and impart a soft, dry feel; mild and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Aluminum Stearate
ingredients.note: Aluminum salt of stearic acid used as a thickener, suspending and emulsion-stabilizing agent; CIR concluded it is safe with low dermal toxicity. Not a toxic-metal contaminant concern at cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Amber Powder
ingredients.note: Fossilized pine resin (amber) milled into powder, used as a mild physical exfoliant and deodorizer. Benign.

ingredients.name: Amethyst Powder
ingredients.note: Finely milled amethyst (a quartz/silica mineral) used as a bulking, abrasive or marketing 'gemstone' powder. Inert and low-hazard topically.

sources.label: Comment on potential carcinogens in makeup cosmetics (crystalline silica context) - PMC

ingredients.name: Aminobutyric Acid
ingredients.note: Non-protein amino acid (GABA) used as a skin-conditioning/anti-wrinkle active claiming muscle-relaxing and moisturizing effects. Skin-identical compound considered safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Aminomethyl Propanediol
ingredients.note: Aliphatic amino-alcohol used as pH adjuster/neutralizer. CIR concluded safe at concentrations up to 2%; buffered in finished products.

ingredients.name: Aminomethyl Propanol
ingredients.note: Amino-alcohol base used to neutralize acrylic thickeners. Considered safe at low neutralizing levels and a common TEA alternative.

ingredients.name: Aminopropyl Ascorbyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Stable water-soluble vitamin C derivative used as an antioxidant and brightening agent. Parent ascorbic acid/ascorbates judged safe by CIR.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/Carboxyethyl Acrylate Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Pre-neutralized crosslinked synthetic polymer used as a thickener/stabilizer and suspending agent over a wide pH range. CIR-reviewed acrylate polymer regarded as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Acryloyldimethyltaurate/VP Copolymer
ingredients.note: Pre-neutralized acrylic copolymer that thickens and stabilizes emulsions at a wide pH. Considered safe and non-sensitizing as used.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Lauryl Sulfate
ingredients.note: Sulfate cleanser similar to SLS; a moderate skin and eye irritant that can be drying. Not ethoxylated, so no 1,4-dioxane concern, and not a toxin or endocrine disruptor.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Polyacrylate
ingredients.note: Ammonium salt of polyacrylic acid used as a thickener/emulsion stabilizer and film former; high-molecular-weight polymer reviewed by CIR as safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Ammonium Polyacryloyldimethyl Taurate
ingredients.note: Synthetic acryloyldimethyltaurate polymer used as a thickener/rheology modifier and emulsion stabilizer; CIR-assessed safe.

ingredients.name: Amodimethicone
ingredients.note: Amino-functionalized polydimethylsiloxane used mainly as a hair-conditioning silicone that films onto the hair shaft. CIR/FDA consider it safe; not irritating or sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Amorphophallus Konjac Root Powder
ingredients.note: Glucomannan-rich milled konjac tuber used as a hydrating, mild exfoliating bulking/texture agent. Generally well tolerated.

sources.label: Amorphophallus konjac: traditional uses, bioactive potential, emerging health applications - PMC
Effect of Konjac Glucomannan on dermal environment against UVB-induced condition - PMC
Beneficial health characteristics of native and hydrolysed konjac glucomannan - PubMed

ingredients.name: Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Pineapple fruit extract containing bromelain enzyme, AHAs and vitamin C; used for gentle enzymatic exfoliation and brightening. Safe at typical cosmetic levels.

Properties and therapeutic application of bromelain (pineapple enzyme) - PMC
Applications of bromelain from pineapple waste towards acne - PMC

ingredients.name: Ananas Sativus (Pineapple) Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Pineapple fruit water (aqueous fraction). Mild hydrating botanical; the same fruit also yields enzymes (bromelain) used for gentle exfoliation.

sources.label: Ananas comosus peels extract as new natural cosmetic ingredient, topical nano cream - PubMed
Properties and therapeutic application of bromelain (pineapple enzyme) - PMC
Applications of bromelain from pineapple waste towards acne - PMC

ingredients.name: Anastatica Hierochuntica Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of the desert resurrection plant (Rose of Jericho) used as a hydrating, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Angelica Acutiloba Root Extract
ingredients.note: Root extract of Japanese angelica used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Standard cosmetic botanical; note that Apiaceae roots can contain trace furocoumarins, but extract is treated as benign at use levels.

sources.label: Anti-photoaging effects of Angelica acutiloba root ethanol extract in human dermal fibroblasts - PubMed

ingredients.name: Angelica Keiskei Extract
ingredients.note: Ashitaba extract (chalcones, flavonoids) used as antioxidant, astringent, emollient and skin-protecting agent.

ingredients.name: Anhydroxylitol
ingredients.note: Cyclic sugar derived from xylitol used as a hygroscopic humectant and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found it non-irritating, non-sensitizing and safe as used.

ingredients.name: Annatto (CI 75120)
ingredients.note: Natural carotenoid colorant from Bixa orellana seeds; FDA-listed color additive exempt from certification, approved in US and EU.

ingredients.name: Anthemis Nobilis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Roman chamomile flower extract used as a soothing botanical. Benign; rare Asteraceae contact allergy possible.

ingredients.name: Anthemis Nobilis Flower Oil
ingredients.note: Roman chamomile essential oil used as fragrance and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found Anthemis nobilis ingredients not irritating or sensitizing as used; Asteraceae-allergic users should be cautious.

ingredients.name: Anthyllis Vulneraria Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Kidney vetch flower extract used as a skin-conditioning/brightening botanical; standard plant extract.

Phenolic profile, EPR determination, and antiproliferative activity of Anthyllis vulneraria extracts - PMC
Comprehensive phytochemical characterization of kidney vetch (Anthyllis vulneraria L.) - PubMed

ingredients.name: Arachidic Acid
ingredients.note: Saturated C20 fatty acid used as an emollient and opacifying/texture agent. Standard low-hazard fatty acid.

ingredients.name: Arachidyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: A C20 fatty alcohol used as an emollient, emulsion stabilizer and thickener; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Arachidyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: An alkyl glucoside (fatty alcohol + glucose) used as a mild O/W emulsifier, typically paired with arachidyl/behenyl alcohol. Benign cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Arctium Lappa Root Extract
ingredients.note: Burdock root extract (inulin, arctiin, polyacetylenes) used as skin-conditioning, soothing and astringent agent; minor allergy risk.

ingredients.name: Argan Oil
ingredients.note: Nutrient-rich emollient oil; benign and well-tolerated on skin and hair.

ingredients.name: Argania Spinosa (Argan) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Argan kernel oil rich in unsaturated fatty acids and vitamin E, used as an emollient and antioxidant. CAS 223747-87-3, listed in EU CosIng.

ingredients.name: Arginine
ingredients.note: Amino acid used as humectant and pH adjuster; benign endogenous compound.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Annua Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet wormwood extract with antioxidant and anti-inflammatory phytochemicals. Used as a soothing botanical; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Capillaris Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Flower extract of capillary wormwood used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical. Asteraceae-family origin means rare contact sensitivity is possible, but treated as benign at use levels.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Princeps Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Mugwort leaf extract used as a soothing/antioxidant botanical. Generally benign topically; Artemisia pollen-allergic individuals may rarely react.

ingredients.name: Artemisia Scoparia Extract
ingredients.note: Whole-plant Artemisia scoparia extract used as a skin-conditioning, soothing antioxidant botanical. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Ascophyllum Nodosum Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (algae) extract used for skin conditioning/antioxidant and moisturizing effects; standard low-risk botanical marine ingredient.

ingredients.name: Ascorbic acid
ingredients.note: Antioxidant and safe brightener. Can mildly irritate at high strength but no safety concern.

ingredients.name: Ascorbyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Glucose-bound vitamin C derivative converted to ascorbic acid in skin for brightening. Stable and well tolerated; safe.

ingredients.name: Ascorbyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: Lipid-soluble vitamin C ester used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. CIR reviewed ascorbic acid derivatives as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Asiatic Acid
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica triterpene aglycone with antioxidant and wound-healing activity; CIR found Centella-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Asiaticoside
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica triterpenoid glycoside that promotes collagen synthesis and calms inflammation; CIR found Centella-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Aspartic Acid
ingredients.note: Endogenous amino acid used as a skin/hair conditioning humectant. Covered by the CIR alpha-amino acids safety assessment as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Astaxanthin
ingredients.note: Potent red carotenoid antioxidant from microalgae used for photo-protection and anti-aging. Safe; may impart a faint color to formulas.

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Murumuru Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian palm seed butter rich in lauric acid, used as occlusive emollient for skin and hair. CIR ruled it safe in current cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Tucumã Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian tucuma seed butter used as an emollient/skin-conditioning fat; benign cosmetic plant butter.

ingredients.name: Astrocaryum Vulgare (Tucumã) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Amazonian palm kernel oil rich in lauric/oleic acids used as an emollient. Benign plant oil.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Bran Extract
ingredients.note: Oat bran extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Extract
ingredients.note: Oat kernel extract rich in beta-glucans and avenanthramides; soothing, anti-inflammatory and hydrating, supports barrier. Generally safe, non-comedogenic.

Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed (doi.org)

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Flour
ingredients.note: Ground oat kernel flour acting as absorbent, bulking and soothing agent; rich in beta-glucan. Well tolerated and non-comedogenic.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Oil
ingredients.note: Emollient oil from oat kernels rich in lipids, valued for soothing and barrier support. Benign and widely used in sensitive-skin products.

Safety Assessment of Avena sativa (Oat)-Derived Ingredients - PubMed

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa (Oat) Kernel Protein
ingredients.note: Oat-derived protein used as a film-forming hair- and skin-conditioning agent. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Avena Sativa Kernel (Oat) Extract
ingredients.note: Oat kernel extract used as a soothing antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical; CIR reviewed Avena sativa-derived ingredients as safe as used. Widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Avobenzone
ingredients.note: Primary broad-spectrum UVA filter, well-studied with low systemic concern. Photo-unstable on its own and requires stabilizers (e.g. octocrylene); some skin absorption seen, hence the light caution.

ingredients.name: Azelaic Acid
ingredients.note: Used in cosmetics up to about 10% for brightening and blemishes; prescription forms reach 15-20%. Cosmetic levels are well tolerated but can cause transient tingling or mild irritation.

ingredients.name: Azeloyl Glycine
ingredients.note: Water-soluble azelaic acid derivative used for brightening, oil control, and soothing. Gentle and well tolerated at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Bacillus Ferment
ingredients.note: Probiotic-derived ferment of Bacillus species providing enzymes and peptides for gentle exfoliation and conditioning. Considered low hazard at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Bacillus/Soybean Ferment Extract
ingredients.note: A ferment of soybeans by Bacillus subtilis (natto), used as a hydrating, film-forming, skin-conditioning active. Standard benign biotech cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Bacopa Monnieri Extract
ingredients.note: Whole-plant extract (brahmi) used as an antioxidant, anti-stress skin-conditioning agent. A standard, low-hazard botanical.

sources.label: Wound healing activity of Bacopa monniera on different wound models in rats - PMC
Bacopa monnieri phytochemicals regulate fibroblast cell migration - PMC
Pharmacological attributes of Bacopa monnieri extract - PMC

ingredients.name: Bakuchiol
ingredients.note: Plant-derived antioxidant marketed as a gentle retinol alternative for smoothing and firming. Generally well tolerated and not photosensitizing, though sourced extracts should be psoralen-free.

ingredients.name: Bambusa Vulgaris Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Bamboo extract (silica-rich) used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Batyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Glyceryl ether of stearyl alcohol used as an emollient, occlusive and emulsion stabilizer. CIR concluded it is safe as used. CAS 544-62-7.

sources.label: Safety assessment of alkyl glyceryl ethers as used in cosmetics - PubMed
Alkylglycerol derivatives as skin penetration modulators - PubMed

ingredients.name: Beeswax
ingredients.note: Natural structuring wax and emollient; safe with only rare sensitivity.

ingredients.name: Beheneth-20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated behenyl alcohol (PEG ether) used as a nonionic emulsifier and surfactant. Standard, well-tolerated.

sources.label: Safety assessment of alkyl PEG ethers as used in cosmetics - PubMed
Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: Behenic Acid
ingredients.note: C22 saturated fatty acid (from rapeseed/peanut oils) used as emulsifier, thickener and opacifier; conditioning to skin and hair.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Fatty Acids & Fatty Acid Salts as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Behentrimonium Methosulfate
ingredients.note: Mild quaternary-ammonium hair/skin conditioning agent reviewed safe by CIR; large molecule with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Behenyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Long-chain fatty alcohol used as emollient and thickener; safe in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Behenyl Behenate
ingredients.note: Wax ester of behenyl alcohol and behenic acid used as an emollient, viscosity/structuring agent in sticks and balms. Benign cosmetic wax.

ingredients.name: Belamcanda Chinensis Root Extract
ingredients.note: Leopard lily root extract (CAS 223748-07-1) rich in isoflavones; used as a soothing, anti-inflammatory skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Bentonite
ingredients.note: Swelling clay used as a thickener and oil absorber; considered safe and non-irritating in topical formulations.

ingredients.name: Benzoic Acid
ingredients.note: Widely used food-grade preservative effective in acidic formulas, EU-permitted up to 0.5% in leave-on. Low sensitization risk, occasional non-immune contact urticaria.

ingredients.name: Benzotriazolyl Dodecyl P-Cresol
ingredients.note: A benzotriazole UV-absorber/light stabilizer (Tinogard TL) added to protect formula colors and fragrances from photo-oxidation, not as a skin sunscreen. Used at low levels and rated low-risk.

Risk assessment of Drometrizole, a cosmetic UV absorber - PMC

ingredients.name: Bertholletia Excelsa (Brazil Nut) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Fatty Brazil nut seed oil used as emollient; standard plant oil (tree-nut allergen caution for sensitized individuals).

ingredients.name: Bertholletia Excelsa Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Brazil nut seed oil rich in unsaturated fatty acids and selenium used as an emollient. Benign plant oil (nut-allergen caution for sensitive users).

ingredients.name: Beta-Carotene
ingredients.note: Orange carotenoid and vitamin A precursor with antioxidant activity. Safe topically; high levels may tint the skin or product.

ingredients.name: Beta-Glucan
ingredients.note: Polysaccharide that hydrates and soothes; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Beta-Sitosterol
ingredients.note: Plant phytosterol used as an emollient and emulsion stabilizer; CIR phytosterols assessment rates it safe.

ingredients.name: Betaine
ingredients.note: Plant-derived osmolyte humectant; soothing, well tolerated and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Betaphycus Gelatinum Extract
ingredients.note: Red seaweed extract rich in carrageenan polysaccharides; used for hydration/film-forming. Covered by CIR red algae assessment.

ingredients.name: Betula Platyphylla Japonica Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Birch bark extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Betula Platyphylla Japonica Juice
ingredients.note: Sap/juice of Japanese white birch used as a hydrating, mineral-rich skin-conditioning aqueous base. Standard benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Bifida Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Bifidobacteria fermentation filtrate used as a barrier-supporting/soothing skin-conditioning active; considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Bifida Ferment Lysate
ingredients.note: Probiotic bacterial lysate used as a skin-conditioning and barrier-supporting active. Generally regarded as low-toxicity and well tolerated, including for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Biosaccharide Gum-1

ingredients.name: Biosaccharide Gum-4

ingredients.name: Bis-Behenyl/Isostearyl/Phytosteryl Dimer Dilinoleyl Dimer Dilinoleate

ingredients.note: Lanolin-like polyester emollient used to give rich emollience and structure. Standard low-hazard cosmetic emollient.

Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Bisabolol

ingredients.name: Bisethylhexyloxyphenol Methoxyphenyl Triazine

ingredients.name: Bismuth Oxychloride

ingredients.name: Bixa Orellana (Achiote) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Achiote seed extract rich in carotenoids (bixin/norbixin) used for color and antioxidant effect; benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Bixa Orellana Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Carotenoid-rich (bixin/norbixin) extract of achiote seeds used for color and antioxidant effect; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Blue 1 (CI 42090)

ingredients.name: Blue 1 Lake (CI 42090)

sources.label: FDA Summary of Color Additives for Use in Foods, Drugs, Cosmetics and Medical Devices

ingredients.name: Borago Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Borage extract rich in omega-6 fatty acids and antioxidants used as an emollient/skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated; rare irritation in those sensitive to vegetable oils.

ingredients.name: Borago Officinalis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Borage seed oil rich in gamma-linolenic acid, used as a conditioning emollient. Benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Boron Nitride
ingredients.note: Plate-like powder prized for soft-focus optics and silky slip; inert and considered safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Brassica Campestris (Rapeseed) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Rapeseed (canola) plant seed oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning fatty oil. Standard vegetable oil, well-tolerated and low sensitization.

ingredients.name: Brassica Campestris (Rapeseed) Sprout Extract
ingredients.note: Rapeseed sprout extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Brassica Campestris (Rapeseed) Sterols
ingredients.note: Rapeseed-derived phytosterols used as emollient/skin-barrier-restoring conditioning agents. CIR phytosterols assessment supports safety; recognized as safe by EU.

ingredients.name: Brassica Oleracea Gemmifera (Brussels Sprouts) Extract
ingredients.note: Brussels sprouts extract used as an antioxidant/conditioning botanical. Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Brassica Oleracea Italica (Broccoli) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Plant seed oil rich in fatty acids used as a lightweight emollient. Considered benign for skin and hair.

ingredients.name: Brassica Oleracea Italica (Broccoli) Sprout Extract
ingredients.note: Broccoli sprout extract used as an antioxidant skin conditioner; rich in vitamins and sulforaphane-type compounds. Low hazard profile.

ingredients.name: Buddleja Officinalis Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Butterfly bush flower extract rich in verbascoside/echinacoside, used as an antioxidant/photoprotective botanical; not on EWG restricted lists.

ingredients.name: Butane
ingredients.note: Liquefied-gas aerosol propellant; chemically inert on skin and low toxicity but extremely flammable and an inhalation hazard from sprays. Precautionary primarily for flammability and inhalation risk.

ingredients.name: Butyl Acetate
ingredients.note: Common nail-lacquer solvent of low chronic toxicity; mainly an inhalation irritant and highly flammable at use concentrations.

ingredients.name: Butyl Avocadate
ingredients.note: Butyl ester of avocado (Persea gratissima) fatty acid used as antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed
Sustainability in skin care: incorporation of avocado peel extracts in topical formulations - PMC

ingredients.name: Butylene Glycol
ingredients.note: Widely used humectant and solvent; low irritation potential and considered safe by CIR. Sensitization is rare.

ingredients.name: Butylene/Ethylene/Styrene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic copolymer used as a viscosity-controlling agent, film-former, and gellant in lipsticks/balms; inert at use levels.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Styrene and Vinyl-type Styrene Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics - CIR

ingredients.name: Butyloctyl Salicylate
ingredients.note: Salicylate ester emollient that solubilizes UV filters and boosts SPF; structurally similar to octisalate. CIR reviewed salicylate esters as safe; EU limits use to ~5%.

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter
ingredients.note: Plant-derived emollient butter rich in fatty acids; widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of shea butter used as a rich, nourishing emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign botanical fat.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Butyrospermum parkii (Shea)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - doi.org
Safety Assessment of Butyrospermum parkii (Shea) - PubMed
Butyrospermum parkii (shea) butter - Cosmetic Ingredient Review

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Butter Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: Concentrated non-saponifiable lipid fraction of shea butter used as an emollient and skin-conditioner. Generally recognized as safe and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Extract
ingredients.note: Shea-derived emollient rich in fatty acids and unsaponifiables. CIR and FDA consider shea safe for cosmetic use.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Butyrospermum parkii (Shea)-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics - doi.org
Safety Assessment of Butyrospermum parkii (Shea) - PubMed
Butyrospermum parkii (shea) butter - Cosmetic Ingredient Review

ingredients.name: Butyrospermum Parkii (Shea) Oil
ingredients.note: Liquid fraction of shea used as a rich emollient/skin-conditioning oil; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: C10-18 Triglycerides
ingredients.note: Blend of medium-to-long-chain triglycerides (often coconut/sunflower-derived) used as an emollient and texture agent. CAS 85665-33-4, well tolerated.

sources.label: Amended Safety Assessment of Triglycerides as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: C11-15 Pareth-7
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated fatty alcohol used as a nonionic surfactant and emulsifier. Standard cosmetic cleansing/emulsifying ingredient.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Alkyl PEG/PPG Ethers as Used in Cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: C12-13 Alketh-9
ingredients.note: An ethoxylated C12-13 alcohol (9 EO) used as a nonionic surfactant/emulsifier and solubilizer. Standard cleansing/emulsifying aid.

sources.label: Safety assessment of alkyl PEG ethers as used in cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: C12-14 Alketh-12
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated C12-14 alcohol used as a nonionic surfactant/emulsifier. Standard benign surfactant.

ingredients.name: C12-14 Isoparaffin
ingredients.note: Branched hydrocarbon used as a solvent and emollient. CIR found isoparaffins non-genotoxic and safe as used.

ingredients.name: C12-15 Alcohols
ingredients.note: A mixture of fatty alcohols (C12-C15) used as emollients, emulsion stabilizers and viscosity agents. Generally non-irritating fatty alcohols.

ingredients.name: C12-15 Alkyl Benzoate
ingredients.note: Lightweight benzoic acid ester used as an emollient and pigment/sunscreen dispersant. CIR found it safe, non-irritating and not a sensitizer at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: C12-15 Alkyl Ethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: Synthetic alkyl ester used as a light emollient and skin conditioner. CAS 90411-66-8, EINECS 291-443-0; standard formulation ingredient.

ingredients.name: C12-16 Alcohols
ingredients.note: Blend of long-chain fatty alcohols used as emulsifiers, thickeners and emollients (not drying like simple alcohols). CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: C12-20 Alkyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived non-ionic emulsifier/surfactant used to stabilize emulsions. Mild, well-tolerated cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: C13-14 Isoparaffin
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived branched hydrocarbon used as an emollient, solvent and texture enhancer. CIR found it safe at 0.0001-75% in 2013.

ingredients.name: C13-15 Alkane
ingredients.note: Plant-derived branched alkane emollient/solvent used as a lightweight silicone or volatile-hydrocarbon alternative. Standard benign emollient.

ingredients.name: C13-16 Isoalkane
ingredients.note: Branched-chain hydrocarbon mixture functioning as a lightweight emollient and solvent. CIR concluded isoparaffins are safe as used.

ingredients.name: C14-22 Alcohols
ingredients.note: Blend of long-chain fatty alcohols used as emollient co-emulsifiers and thickeners (not drying ethanol). Standard, mild cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: C15-19 Alkane
ingredients.note: Synthetic (often plant-derived) hydrocarbon mixture used as a lightweight emollient and silicone/mineral-oil substitute. Considered non-irritating and non-sensitizing at cosmetic use levels.

sources.label: Safety assessment of isoparaffins as used in cosmetics - PubMed

ingredients.name: C18-21 Alkane
ingredients.note: Biodegradable C18-21 alkane blend used as a non-polar emollient/solvent. Benign standard emollient.

ingredients.name: C20-24 Alkyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Long-chain alkyl-modified silicone used as an emollient/water-resistant film former; silicones regarded as safe, low-irritation cosmetic ingredients.

sources.label: PubMed: Final safety assessment of dimethicone and methicone ingredients
CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Dimethicone, Methicone, and Substituted-

ingredients.name: C20-40 Alcohols
ingredients.note: Mixture of long-chain (C20-C40) fatty alcohols used as a thickener, co-emulsifier and emollient. Fatty alcohols are CIR-reviewed and safe as used.

sources.label: EU CosIng ingredient database (C20-40 Alkyl Stearate related entry)
CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Alkyl Esters as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: C30-45 Alkyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Long-chain alkyl-modified silicone used as an emollient/film-forming texture enhancer; CIR concluded it is safe as used, non-irritating and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: C8-12 Acid Triglyceride
ingredients.note: Coconut/palm-kernel-derived medium-chain triglyceride used as a light, low-comedogenicity emollient; CIR concluded glyceryl triesters safe.

ingredients.name: C9-12 Alkane
ingredients.note: Light hydrocarbon (often plant-derived) alkane mixture used as a volatile solvent/emollient and silicone alternative. Standard, benign carrier.

ingredients.name: Caesalpinia Sappan Stem Powder
ingredients.note: Sappanwood-derived powder (brazilin/flavonoids) used as an antioxidant, brightening, soothing botanical; benign plant material.

sources.label: PubMed: Traditional Uses and Phytochemical and Pharmacological Analyses of Caesalpinia sappan
PubMed: Protective role of Caesalpinia sappan extract and its main component
PubMed: Brazilin and Caesalpinia sappan L. extract protect epidermal keratinocytes from oxidative stress

ingredients.name: Caesalpinia Spinosa Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Tara-derived polysaccharide-rich extract used as a film-former and texturizer. Studies show low irritation, no genotoxicity and minimal comedogenicity.

ingredients.name: Caffeine
ingredients.note: Methylxanthine used as an antioxidant, vasoconstrictor and anti-puffiness active. Considered safe topically; not significantly absorbed at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Calcium Aluminum Borosilicate
ingredients.note: Glass-type platelet used as a non-plastic glitter and shimmer base; inert on skin. Cosmetic glitters overall face EU microplastic restrictions, but borosilicate is a compliant alternative.

ingredients.name: Calcium Carbonate
ingredients.note: Mineral powder used as a bulking and oil-absorbing filler in powders; inert and safe topically.

ingredients.name: Calcium Chloride
ingredients.note: An inorganic salt used as a viscosity/texture modifier and ionic crosslinker. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Calcium Gluconate
ingredients.note: Calcium salt of gluconic acid used as a buffering and skin-conditioning agent; also a recognized pharmaceutical/food calcium source. Well-tolerated and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Calcium Lactate
ingredients.note: Calcium salt of lactic acid used as a buffering/skin-conditioning agent. CIR found it safe up to 10% at pH >=3.5 with sun-protection guidance, as for other AHA-related ingredients.

ingredients.name: Calcium Pantothenate
ingredients.note: Stable calcium salt of vitamin B5 used as a humectant and skin/hair-conditioning agent supporting barrier repair. Non-irritating and well tolerated.

sources.label: PubMed: Calcium pantothenate is present in cosmetics and may cause contact allergy
CIR: Safety Assessment of Panthenol, Pantothenic Acid, and related ingredients

ingredients.name: Calcium Sodium Borosilicate
ingredients.note: Inert glass flake used as bulking/sparkle agent and pigment substrate. CIR concluded borosilicate glasses safe; non-irritating in patch tests.

ingredients.name: Calendula Extract
ingredients.note: Triterpene-rich soothing extract used for irritated and compromised skin; generally benign, with only rare Asteraceae-related allergy.

ingredients.name: Callicarpa Japonica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Fruit extract of Japanese beautyberry used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign cosmetic plant extract.

sources.label: PubMed: Callicarpa japonica Thunb. attenuates cigarette smoke-induced effects
PMC: Biologically Active Natural Products of the Genus Callicarpa

ingredients.name: Calophyllum Inophyllum Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Tamanu seed oil used as a barrier-repairing emollient with anti-inflammatory/wound-healing properties; reported non-sensitizing and non-comedogenic.

ingredients.name: Camellia Japonica Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Camellia (tsubaki) flower extract used as a conditioning/antioxidant botanical. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Camellia Japonica Flower Water
ingredients.note: Camellia (tsubaki) flower hydrosol. Mild antioxidant, hydrating botanical water common in K-beauty toners.

sources.label: PubMed: Anti-photoaging capability of antioxidant extract from Camellia japonica leaf
PMC: Phytochemical Insights and Industrial Applications of Camellia japonica Leaves
PubMed: Protective effects of Camellia japonica flower extract

ingredients.name: Camellia Japonica Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Leaf extract of Japanese camellia, used as an antioxidant skin conditioner. CAS 223748-13-8, listed in EU CosIng.

sources.label: PubMed: Anti-photoaging capability of antioxidant extract from Camellia japonica leaf
PMC: Phytochemical Insights and Industrial Applications of Camellia japonica Leaves
PubMed: Protective effects of Camellia japonica flower extract

ingredients.name: Camellia Japonica Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Lightweight emollient seed oil rich in oleic/linoleic acids, vitamin E and squalene; low comedogenicity.

ingredients.name: Camellia Kissi Seed Oil
ingredients.note: High-oleic plant oil from Camellia kissi seeds used as a lightweight emollient and skin/hair conditioner. Benign cosmetic oil.

sources.label: CIR: Camellia kissi seed oil ingredient page
PMC: Characterization and phylogenetic significance of the complete chloroplast genome of Camellia kissii
PubMed: Lipid Characteristics of Camellia Seed Oil

ingredients.name: Camellia Oleifera Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Tea-plant leaf extract rich in polyphenols/catechins used as antioxidant and soothing agent. Benign, suited to sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Camellia Oleifera Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Oleic-acid-rich plant oil used as a non-comedogenic emollient and antioxidant; reviewed by CIR.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sativa Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Omega-3-rich seed oil (commonly the camelina species) used as a lightweight emollient and antioxidant carrier oil.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sinensis (Green Tea) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Green tea leaf extract rich in catechin polyphenols, used as antioxidant and skin conditioner. CIR concluded it is safe (up to ~0.86% leave-on) when formulated to be nonsensitizing.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sinensis (White Tea) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Polyphenol-rich tea leaf extract used as an antioxidant and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found Camellia sinensis-derived ingredients safe when formulated to be nonsensitizing.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sinensis Leaf Powder
ingredients.note: Green tea leaf powder used as antioxidant, skin-conditioning agent and mild exfoliant; catechins (EGCG) provide free-radical scavenging.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sinensis Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Green tea leaf water (aqueous distillate). Carries antioxidant polyphenols; soothing and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Camellia Sinensis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Cold-pressed green tea seed oil used as an emollient; CIR has found it safe in current cosmetic uses. Non-comedogenic and rich in fatty acids and vitamin E.

ingredients.name: Candelilla Cera/Euphorbia Cerifera (Candelilla) Wax
ingredients.note: Plant-derived structuring wax from the candelilla shrub, used to thicken and harden balms and sticks. CIR found it safe as used with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Candelilla Cera/Euphorbia Cerifera (Candelilla) Wax/Cire De Candelilla
ingredients.note: Plant wax used as a structuring/texturizing and film-forming agent. CIR found it safe; may carry trace benzyl alcohol requiring declaration.

ingredients.name: Candelilla Wax
ingredients.note: Plant-derived structuring wax; benign and commonly used in balms.

ingredients.name: Candida Bombicola/Glucose/Methyl Rapeseedate Ferment
ingredients.note: Yeast-fermented sophorolipid biosurfactant used as a gentle, biodegradable cleansing/emulsifying agent. No notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Cannabis Sativa Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Hemp seed oil rich in omega fatty acids; contains negligible cannabinoids and functions as an emollient. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Caproic Acid
ingredients.note: Medium-chain fatty acid (hexanoic acid, CAS 142-62-1) used as an emollient/surfactant building block and fragrance/flavor component. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Caprooyl Phytosphingosine
ingredients.note: Sphingolipid ceramide-precursor used as a barrier-replenishing skin-conditioning agent; judged safe by CIR (2020). Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Caprooyl Sphingosine
ingredients.note: A synthetic ceramide-type lipid that reinforces the skin barrier and hydrates. CIR reviewed ceramide ingredients as safe at current cosmetic use levels.

ingredients.name: Caprylhydroxamic Acid
ingredients.note: Coconut-derived chelating/biostatic preservative effective across pH 4-8. CIR concludes it is safe at current concentrations, though it cautions against use on damaged skin.

ingredients.name: Caprylic/Capric Triglyceride
ingredients.note: Lightweight, very stable emollient derived from coconut/palm; non-irritating and widely used.

ingredients.name: Caprylic/Capric/Myristic/Stearic Triglyceride
ingredients.note: Mixed fatty-acid triglyceride used as a light, stable emollient and skin conditioner. Benign, common cosmetic oil.

sources.label: CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Triglycerides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Caprylic/Capric/Stearic Triglyceride
ingredients.note: A blend of medium- and long-chain triglycerides derived from coconut/palm fatty acids and glycerin, used as a lightweight emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe and non-irritating in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Caprylic/Capric/Succinic Triglyceride
ingredients.note: Mixed glycerin triester (coconut-derived) used as a lightweight emollient oil; CIR triglyceride family assessed safe, non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Capryloyl Glycerin/Sebacic Acid Copolymer
ingredients.note: Biodegradable semisynthetic copolymer (coconut/castor-derived) used as silicone-alternative emollient and film former. Benign skin/hair conditioner.

sources.label: EU CosIng: Capryloyl Glycerin/Sebacic Acid Copolymer entry

ingredients.name: Caprylyl Glyceryl Ether
ingredients.note: Multifunctional alkyl glyceryl ether used as an antimicrobial preservative-booster, emollient and humectant. CIR confirmed safety of alkyl glyceryl ethers.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety assessment of alkyl glyceryl ethers as used in cosmetics
CIR: Caprylyl Glyceryl Ether ingredient page

ingredients.name: Caprylyl glycol
ingredients.note: Multifunctional, gentle; safe.

ingredients.name: Caprylyl Methicone
ingredients.note: Volatile alkyl silicone used as a lightweight emollient and spreading agent. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic silicone.

ingredients.name: Caprylyl/Capryl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Short-chain sugar-based nonionic surfactant valued as a mild solubilizer and co-surfactant. Low irritation and good skin tolerance.

ingredients.name: Caramel
ingredients.note: Brown colorant made by heating sugars; FDA color additive exempt from certification and EU Annex IV permitted. CIR deferred to FDA safety assessment.

sources.label: PubMed: Caramel color safety – An update

ingredients.name: Carbomer
ingredients.note: Crosslinked polyacrylic acid that thickens water phases into clear gels when neutralized. Inert, non-irritating and very widely used.

ingredients.name: Carica Papaya (Papaya) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Papaya fruit extract containing papain enzyme, vitamins and AHAs; used as mild exfoliant and skin-conditioning agent. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Carica Papaya (Papaya) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Papaya leaf extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Carica Papaya (Papaya) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Fatty plant seed oil used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent; standard low-risk botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Carmine
ingredients.note: Natural red pigment from cochineal insects. A known allergen that can cause contact dermatitis and rare anaphylaxis in sensitized individuals; not toxic.

ingredients.name: Carnauba Wax
ingredients.note: Hard plant wax used to harden and add gloss; safe cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Carnosine
ingredients.note: Dipeptide antioxidant that protects against glycation and oxidative stress. Safe and non-irritating at cosmetic concentrations.

ingredients.name: Carthamus Tinctorius (Safflower) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Plant flower extract used as a skin-conditioning and antioxidant botanical. Benign botanical with no significant sensitization concern.

ingredients.name: Carthamus Tinctorius (Safflower) Oleosomes
ingredients.note: Microencapsulated safflower seed-oil bodies (oil + protein/phospholipid shell + tocopherol) acting as a natural emollient and time-release delivery system. Benign.

ingredients.name: Carthamus Tinctorius (Safflower) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Linoleic-acid-rich plant seed oil used as an emollient. Generally well tolerated; classified as an oil rather than extract.

ingredients.name: Caryodendron Orinocense (Cacay) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Seed oil rich in linoleic acid and vitamin E used as an emollient/antioxidant. Benign plant oil.

ingredients.name: Cassia Obtusifolia Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of Cassia obtusifolia (sicklepod) seeds used as a soothing, film-forming, skin-conditioning botanical. Standard benign botanical in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Castanea Crenata (Chestnut) Shell Extract
ingredients.note: Polyphenol- and tannin-rich extract of Japanese chestnut inner shell used for antioxidant, brightening and pore-refining effects.

sources.label: PubMed: Antioxidant effects of the chestnut (Castanea crenata) inner shell extract in HepG2 cells
PMC: In Vitro Anti-Malassezia Activity of Castanea crenata Shell
PubMed: Metabolic Profiling of Chestnut Shell (Castanea crenata) Cultivars

ingredients.name: Castanea Sativa (Chestnut) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Chestnut seed extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Castor Oil/IPDI Copolymer
ingredients.note: Castor oil reacted with isophorone diisocyanate to form a film-forming/thickening polymer; reacted polymer with negligible free-monomer concern.

ingredients.name: Cellulose Gum
ingredients.note: Carboxymethylcellulose used as a thickener, stabilizer and film former. FDA-recognized GRAS and considered non-irritating in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Centaurea Cyanus Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Cornflower extract used as a soothing, astringent, anti-inflammatory botanical, common in eye-care products. Mild profile with very low irritation/sensitization risk.

sources.label: PubMed: Chemical study, antioxidant, anti-hypertensive, and cytotoxic activities of Centaurea cyanus L. petals aqueous extract
PMC: A Cornflower Extract Containing N-Feruloylserotonin Reduces Inflammation in Human Skin
doi.org: A New, Cost-Effective Facial Skin Care Serum Rich in Bioactive Ingredients from Centaurea cyanus L.

ingredients.name: Centella Asiatica Flower/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Triterpenoid-rich Centella (cica) extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning/antioxidant botanical. CIR concluded Centella-derived ingredients safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Centella Asiatica Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Gotu kola (cica) leaf extract used as a soothing, barrier-supporting botanical; CIR found little sensitization concern at typical use levels when non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Centella Asiatica Leaf Water
ingredients.note: Centella (cica) leaf water. Soothing, barrier-supportive botanical hydrosol popular in calming toners.

ingredients.name: Centella Asiatica Root Extract
ingredients.note: Gotu kola root extract used as soothing skin-conditioning agent. CIR concluded safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing; may occasionally irritate.

ingredients.name: Cera Alba/Beeswax
ingredients.note: Natural beeswax used as a thickener, emollient and structuring/film-forming agent. CIR-assessed safe; very low sensitization risk.

ingredients.name: Cera Carnauba/Copernicia Cerifera (Carnauba) Wax/Cire De Carnauba
ingredients.note: Palm-derived wax used as a film-forming/texturizing agent. CIR concluded safe with low irritation/comedogenic potential.

ingredients.name: Cera Microcristallina/Microcrystalline Wax/Cire Microcristalline
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived wax used as a binder, thickener and structuring agent in sticks and balms. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Ceramide AP
ingredients.note: Phytosphingosine-based ceramide that reinforces the stratum corneum barrier. CIR deemed ceramides safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Ceramide AS
ingredients.note: Skin-identical ceramide (alpha-hydroxy fatty acid + sphingosine base) used to restore the stratum corneum barrier and reduce water loss. CIR-assessed class, well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Ceramide EOP
ingredients.note: Ester-linked omega-hydroxy ceramide essential to skin barrier lamellar structure. CIR assessed ceramides as safe for use in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Ceramide EOS
ingredients.note: Skin-identical ceramide lipid used to support the barrier and reduce water loss; judged safe by CIR. Benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Ceramide NP
ingredients.note: Skin-identical barrier lipid; safe and beneficial.

ingredients.name: Ceramide NS
ingredients.note: Skin-identical sphingolipid that reinforces the stratum corneum barrier and reduces transepidermal water loss; CIR-assessed as safe.

ingredients.name: Ceratonia Siliqua (Carob) Gum
ingredients.note: Carob (locust bean) gum, a natural polysaccharide used as a thickener and stabilizer; widely used and benign.

sources.label: CIR: Final Report on the Safety Assessment of Galactomannans as Used in Cosmetics
PubMed: Locust bean gum: processing, properties and food applications – a review

ingredients.name: Cereus Grandiflorus (Cactus) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Cactus (night-blooming cereus) flower extract used as a hydrating, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. No significant safety concerns in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Ceteareth-25
ingredients.note: Nonionic ethoxylated cetearyl alcohol emulsifier/surfactant. CIR considers ceteareth ingredients safe when purified to limit 1,4-dioxane and ethylene oxide residues.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Blend of cetyl and stearyl alcohols; standard emulsifier and emollient, safe and non-drying.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Ethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: Light synthetic ester emollient providing slip and spreadability. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived (glucose) nonionic emulsifier blended with cetearyl alcohol. Mild, biodegradable and well tolerated; a common 'green' alternative to PEG emulsifiers.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Isononanoate
ingredients.note: Fatty-alcohol ester of isononanoic acid used as a light, non-greasy emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Cetearyl Olivate
ingredients.note: Olive-derived emulsifying ester (often paired with sorbitan olivate); safe and skin-friendly.

ingredients.name: Cetraria Islandica Extract
ingredients.note: Iceland moss (lichen) extract whose polysaccharides (lichenin) provide humectant/film-forming soothing benefits; contains some usnic acid for mild antimicrobial action. Low reported topical irritation.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Fatty (non-drying) alcohol used as emollient and thickener; benign and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Diglyceryl Tris(Trimethylsiloxy)Silylethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A silicone-based nonionic emulsifier used to form and stabilize water-in-silicone emulsions. Inert, low-hazard formulation aid.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of Dimethicone, Methicone, and Substituted dimethicone

ingredients.name: Cetyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Alkyl-modified silicone (liquid silicone wax) used as emollient and skin-conditioning agent. CIR alkoxy/alkyl polysiloxane assessments support cosmetic safety.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Esters
ingredients.note: Wax-like emollient esters used for texture; benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Ethylhexanoate
ingredients.note: Light fatty-alcohol ester emollient giving smooth spreadability; CIR-reviewed alkyl ester considered safe.

ingredients.name: Cetyl Hydroxyethylcellulose
ingredients.note: Hydrophobically-modified cellulose used as a thickener, film-former and emulsion stabilizer. Standard, well-tolerated texture agent.

sources.label: PubMed: Safety of ingredients used in cosmetics

ingredients.name: Cetyl PEG/PPG-10/1 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-based water-in-oil emulsifier widely used in creams. Standard low-hazard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Chaenomeles Sinensis Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Chinese quince fruit extract used as an antioxidant, humectant and skin conditioner; contains flavonoids and organic acids. Low hazard profile.

ingredients.name: Chamaecyparis Obtusa Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Hinoki (Japanese cypress) leaf extract used as an antioxidant, antimicrobial and soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Chamomile Extract
ingredients.note: Bisabolol- and chamazulene-rich anti-inflammatory extract; well tolerated, though Asteraceae-allergic individuals may rarely react.

ingredients.name: Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: German chamomile flower extract used as a soothing/anti-inflammatory botanical. Benign, though rare contact allergy possible in Asteraceae-sensitive individuals.

ingredients.name: Chamomilla Recutita (Matricaria) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: German chamomile leaf extract used as a soothing, anti-inflammatory antioxidant botanical. Asteraceae-family origin carries rare contact-allergy potential but is generally benign.

sources.label: CIR: Amended Safety Assessment of Chamomilla Recutita-Derived Ingredients
PMC: In Vitro Safety and Efficacy Evaluation of a Juniperus communis Callus and Matricaria recutita Extract Combination

ingredients.name: Charcoal Powder
ingredients.note: Plant-derived activated charcoal used as an absorbent, abrasive, opacifier and black colorant; CIR reviewed plant-derived charcoal ingredients. Poses no notable hazard in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Chlorella Ferment
ingredients.note: Microalgae-derived ferment rich in peptides and amino acids used as a skin-conditioning/antioxidant active. Generally well tolerated; high iodine content can be a concern for very sensitive or acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Chlorella Vulgaris Extract
ingredients.note: Green microalgae extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant botanical. Benign.

ingredients.name: Chlorphenesin
ingredients.note: Well-tolerated preservative permitted in the EU up to 0.3%. Occasional mild irritation but a rare sensitizer.

ingredients.name: Cholesterol
ingredients.note: Skin-identical lipid used as emollient and barrier-repair/emulsion stabilizer; benign endogenous sterol.

ingredients.name: Chondrus Crispus
ingredients.note: Red seaweed (Irish moss) used as a natural thickening/gelling and skin-conditioning agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Chondrus Crispus Extract
ingredients.note: Red algae (Irish moss) extract used as a hydrating, film-forming and thickening agent. Standard low-hazard marine botanical.

ingredients.name: Chondrus Crispus Powder
ingredients.note: Red-algae (Irish moss/carrageenan) powder used as a hydrating thickener/gelling and film-forming agent. CIR found red algae-derived ingredients safe as used.

ingredients.name: Chromium Cobalt Aluminum Oxide
ingredients.note: Cobalt-containing spinel blue-green pigment. Generally inert and approved, but cobalt content warrants a precautionary flag for sensitive or cobalt-allergic users.

ingredients.name: Chromium Hydroxide Green
ingredients.note: Hydrated chromium(III) oxide, a blue-green insoluble pigment. Approved for cosmetic use except lips (FDA); inert trivalent chromium is considered safe.

ingredients.name: Chromium Oxide Greens
ingredients.note: Anhydrous chromium(III) oxide, an insoluble inert green pigment. Approved for cosmetics including eye area (FDA prohibits in lip products); the inert trivalent form is not the toxic hexavalent chromium.

ingredients.name: CI 14700 (Red 4)
ingredients.note: FDA-approved red azo dye permitted for externally applied cosmetics (not lips/eye area). Safe at regulated levels topically; rare azo-dye sensitivity.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – FD&C Red No. 4
FDA: Color Additives Permitted for Use in Cosmetics
PMC: Food Safety and Health Concerns of Synthetic Food Colors: An Update

ingredients.name: CI 15850/Red 6, Red 7, Red 7 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA-approved synthetic red dye/lake widely used in lipsticks and color cosmetics. SCCS did not consider it sensitizing; rare contact-allergy reports.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – D&C Red No. 7
FDA: Summary of Color Additives for Use in the United States

ingredients.name: CI 15985 (Yellow 6)
ingredients.note: FDA/EU-approved orange-yellow azo dye colorant. Considered safe at regulated cosmetic levels; rare contact allergy in azo-dye-sensitive individuals.

sources.label: PMC: Safety and efficacy of Sunset Yellow FCF as a feed additive
FDA: How Safe are Color Additives?

ingredients.name: CI 15985/Yellow 6 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA-certified azo dye (Sunset Yellow) aluminum lake used as cosmetic colorant. Safe at approved levels but azo dyes can rarely cause contact allergy; eye-area use requires certification.

ingredients.name: CI 19140/Yellow 5 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA-approved tartrazine azo dye aluminum lake used as cosmetic colorant. Safe topically but can rarely cause itching/hives in sensitive individuals; always labeled.

ingredients.name: CI 19140/Yellow 5, Yellow 5 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA/EU-approved yellow azo dye colorant. Safe at regulated cosmetic levels; uncommon hives in aspirin-intolerant/azo-sensitive individuals.

sources.label: PubMed: Lack of genotoxicity in vivo for food color additive Tartrazine
FDA: Color Additives and Cosmetics: Fact Sheet
PMC: Exposures to FD&C synthetic color additives from over-the-counter medications

ingredients.name: CI 42090/Blue 1 Lake
ingredients.note: Aluminum lake of FD&C Blue 1, an FDA- and CIR-approved triarylmethane colorant with very low skin absorption. Considered safe for topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – FD&C Blue No. 1
FDA: Color Additives Permitted for Use in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: CI 45410/Red 28 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA-certified xanthene (phloxine) aluminum lake colorant used in lip and face makeup. Safe within allowances; some may experience lip/skin irritation, eye-area use restricted.

ingredients.name: CI 73360/Red 30 Lake
ingredients.note: FDA-certified thioindigo (Red 30) lake colorant used in lip and makeup products. Safe per FDA when certified; not permitted in eye-area products.

ingredients.name: CI 77007/Ultramarines
ingredients.note: An inorganic sodium-aluminum-silicate blue/violet pigment approved for cosmetic color use including eye area. Considered safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: CI 77163/Bismuth Oxychloride
ingredients.note: An inorganic pearlescent white pigment; FDA-listed color additive exempt from certification, CIR deferred to FDA. Occasionally reported to cause irritation/sensitization in some users.

ingredients.name: CI 77288/Chromium Oxide Greens
ingredients.note: Trivalent chromium (Cr2O3) green mineral pigment approved for cosmetics; restricted from lip products. Inert insoluble pigment, considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: CI 77289/Chromium Hydroxide Green
ingredients.note: Hydrated trivalent chromium green mineral pigment approved for cosmetics; restricted from lip products. Inert insoluble pigment, considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: CI 77491/Iron Oxides
ingredients.note: FDA-approved inorganic iron oxide pigment (red) used as a colorant in cosmetics. Considered safe and inert.

ingredients.name: CI 77499/Iron Oxides
ingredients.note: Inorganic iron oxide pigment (black, CI 77499) used for color in makeup. FDA-approved color additive, considered safe.

ingredients.name: CI 77510 (Ferric Ferrocyanide)
ingredients.note: Inorganic iron-based blue pigment (Prussian blue), FDA-approved for cosmetics except lips. Does not penetrate skin; considered non-toxic.

sources.label: PubMed: Prussian Blue: A Safe Pigment with Zeolitic-Like Activity
PubMed: In vitro and in vivo genotoxicity assessment of ferric ferrocyanide

ingredients.name: CI 77510/Ferric Ferrocyanide, Ferric Ammonium Ferrocyanide
ingredients.note: Inorganic iron blue pigment (Prussian blue); the ammonium form is the EU-permitted variant. FDA-approved for cosmetics except lips; does not penetrate skin.

sources.label: FDA: Regulatory Status of Color Additives – Ferric Ammonium Ferrocyanide
PubMed: In vitro and in vivo genotoxicity assessment of ferric ferrocyanide

ingredients.name: CI 77742/Manganese Violet
ingredients.note: Inorganic manganese ammonium pyrophosphate pigment approved by FDA and EU (Annex IV) for cosmetic coloring, including eye and lip area, when meeting specifications.

ingredients.name: CI 77891/Titanium Dioxide
ingredients.note: Mineral UV filter, white pigment and opacifier. Topically safe; inhalation of loose powder is the only flagged concern, not relevant to creams/lotions.

ingredients.name: Citric Acid
ingredients.note: Most often a pH adjuster but acts as an AHA exfoliant at higher concentrations and low pH. Exfoliating use increases sun sensitivity and can irritate.

ingredients.name: Citrullus Lanatus (Watermelon) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Watermelon seed extract used as an antioxidant, fatty-acid-rich skin-conditioning botanical. No notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Citrullus Lanatus (Watermelon) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Fast-absorbing watermelon seed oil used as an emollient. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Amara (Bitter Orange) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Bitter-orange blossom (neroli) extract; aqueous extracts are essentially allergen-free but fragrant grades carry linalool/limonene, so flagged precautionary.

ingredients.name: Citrus Aurantium Dulcis (Orange) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Sweet orange fruit extract used as a skin-conditioning antioxidant botanical; fruit extracts are not phototoxic like expressed peel oils. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Citrus Limon Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon fruit extract used as antioxidant/astringent. CIR found fruit extracts non-sensitizing; phototoxicity concern relates to peel essential oils, not the fruit extract.

ingredients.name: Cladosiphon Okamuranus Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of an edible brown seaweed (mozuku), a source of fucoidan, used as a conditioning humectant agent. Benign botanical.

ingredients.name: Coccinia Indica Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Ivy gourd fruit extract used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Coco-Caprylate
ingredients.note: Lightweight plant-derived ester often used as a natural silicone alternative; safe and well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Coco-Glucoside
ingredients.note: Alkyl polyglucoside from coconut fatty alcohols and glucose. Gentle nonionic surfactant/co-emulsifier considered low-irritation.

ingredients.name: Cocoa Butter
ingredients.note: Solid occlusive butter; benign and widely used, though comedogenic for some acne-prone skin.

ingredients.name: Cocoglucosides Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride
ingredients.note: Glucoside-derived quaternized surfactant used for mild cleansing and conditioning; low-hazard cosmetic surfactant.

sources.label: CIR: Guar Hydroxypropyltrimonium Chloride ingredient page
CIR: Safety Assessment of Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Cocoglycerides
ingredients.note: Coconut-derived mono-/di-/triglyceride mixture used as an emollient and texture agent. Standard benign lipid.

sources.label: CIR: Safety Assessment of PEGylated Alkyl Glycerides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Coconut Acid
ingredients.note: Mixture of fatty acids from coconut oil used to make soaps/surfactants and as an emulsifier. CIR reviewed coconut-derived fatty acids as safe for cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Coconut Alkanes
ingredients.note: Plant-derived hydrocarbon emollient used as a light, volatile silicone/dimethicone alternative. No notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Coconut Oil
ingredients.note: Occlusive emollient oil; safe and benign, though comedogenic for some acne-prone facial skin.

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Fruit Water
ingredients.note: Coconut water (aqueous fruit fraction). Lightweight hydrating humectant; distinct from heavier coconut oil.

ingredients.name: Cocos Nucifera (Coconut) Oil
ingredients.note: Plant-derived emollient and occlusive oil widely used in skin and hair care. CIR reviewed plant oils including coconut oil as safe; comedogenic for some users.

ingredients.name: Cocoyl Hydrolyzed Collagen
ingredients.note: Coconut-acid-acylated hydrolyzed collagen used as a mild cleansing/conditioning surfactant; CIR-reviewed, low-hazard.

ingredients.name: Codium Fragile Extract
ingredients.note: Green seaweed extract used as a skin-conditioning/moisturizing agent. A standard, low-hazard marine botanical.

ingredients.name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Leaf/Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Coffee leaf/seed extract used as a botanical antioxidant/skin-conditioning agent; standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Coffea Arabica (Coffee) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Coffee seed extract used as an antioxidant (caffeine, chlorogenic acids) and skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated botanical.

ingredients.name: Coix Lacryma-Jobi Ma-Yuen Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Job's tears (adlay) seed extract rich in amino acids and polysaccharides used as a hydrating, soothing, brightening botanical. Patch test advised for grain-allergic individuals.

ingredients.name: Collagen Amino Acid
ingredients.note: Amino acids derived from hydrolyzed collagen used as a skin-identical humectant/moisturizer. Benign cosmetic ingredient (CAS 9015-54-7).

sources.label: PubMed: Safety assessment of animal- and plant-derived amino acids as used in cosmetics
CIR: Safety Assessment of Skin and Connective Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides
CIR: Safety Assessment of Tissue-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Collagen Amino Acids
ingredients.note: Amino-acid mixture from hydrolyzed collagen used as a humectant and skin/hair conditioning agent. CIR found amino acids safe as used.

ingredients.name: Collagen Extract
