ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Coco-Glycerides
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated coconut-derived glycerides used as an emollient, consistency and texture agent. Standard benign lipid.

sources.label: Final Report on the Safety Assessment of Lard Glyceride and Hydrogenated Lard Glyceride (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Coconut Oil
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated coconut oil used as an emollient and texture-stabilizing skin-conditioning oil with improved oxidative stability. Benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Didecene
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated decene-dimer hydrocarbon used as a lightweight emollient and skin-conditioning film former. CAS 68649-11-6.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Polyene Group as Used in Cosmetics – CIR Final Report (PDF)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Lecithin
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated phospholipid used as a skin-conditioning emulsifier; CIR-reviewed lecithin derivative considered safe.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Microcrystalline Wax
ingredients.note: Refined hydrogenated petroleum-derived wax used as a thickener, structurant and binder in sticks and balms. Inert and regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Olive Oil Stearyl Esters
ingredients.note: Olive-oil-derived ester wax used as an emollient/texturizing skin-conditioning agent; standard low-risk lipid ingredient.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Olive Oil Unsaponifiables
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated non-saponifiable olive fraction used as emollient and silicone-replacement skin/hair conditioner. Standard benign cosmetic lipid.

sources.label: Hydrogenated olive oil unsaponifiables ingredient page – CIR
Safety Assessment of Olea europaea (Olive)-Derived Ingredients – CIR Final Report (PDF)
Vegetable Butters and Oils as Therapeutically and Cosmetically Active Ingredients: A Review of Clinical Studies (PMC)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Phosphatidylcholine
ingredients.note: Skin-identical phospholipid used as emulsifier and liposome/penetration aid; CIR-assessed in 2020 with no concerns.

sources.label: Final report on the safety assessment of Lecithin and Hydrogenated Lecithin (PubMed)
Effect of phosphatidylcholine on skin permeation from gel prepared with hydrogenated phospholipid (PubMed)
Safety Assessment of Lecithin and Other Phosphoglycerides as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Poly(C6-14 Olefin)
ingredients.note: Synthetic hydrocarbon emollient providing a light, non-greasy conditioning film. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Polycyclopentadiene
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated hydrocarbon resin used as a long-wear film former improving adhesion, water-resistance and gloss. Standard texture ingredient.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Polyene Group as Used in Cosmetics – CIR Final Report (PDF)
Hydrogenated polycyclopentadiene resin – PubChem patent record

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Polydecene
ingredients.note: Synthetic hydrocarbon emollient used as a light, stable conditioning oil. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Polyisobutene
ingredients.note: Synthetic hydrocarbon emollient used as a light, non-greasy skin-conditioning oil and lip-gloss base. Considered safe as used by CIR.

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Rice Bran Oil
ingredients.note: Hydrogenated rice bran oil used as an emollient and thickening/texturizing agent for occlusive moisturizing skin feel. Standard benign cosmetic lipid.

sources.label: Hydrogenated rice bran oil ingredient page – CIR
Renewing the potential of rice crop residues as value-added products in the cosmetics industry (PubMed)
Anti-skin ageing activities of rice (Oryza sativa) bran waxes in cultured skin cells (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Starch Hydrolysate
ingredients.note: Polyol mixture from hydrolyzed starch used as a humectant and film former. Mild, suitable for most skin types.

sources.label: Safety assessment of hydrogenated starch hydrolysates (PubMed)
The effect of hydrogenated starch hydrolysates on plaque pH in vivo (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Styrene/Isoprene Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic polymer used as a viscosity controller and film former. CIR concluded it safe as used; styrene unlikely to be absorbed due to molecular size.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Styrene and Vinyl-type Styrene Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)
Studies of Sulfonated Hydrogenated Styrene-Isoprene-Styrene Block Copolymer – Cytotoxicity and Platelet Characteristics (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Hydrogenated Vegetable Oil
ingredients.note: Hardened plant oil used as a structuring agent and emollient. Safe and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Conchiolin Protein
ingredients.note: Pearl/mollusk-shell-derived hydrolyzed protein used as a skin- and hair-conditioning agent. No notable safety concerns.

sources.label: Hydrolyzed Conchiolin Protein (HCP) Extracted from Pearls Antagonizes ET-1 and α-MSH for Skin Whitening (PubMed)
Hydrolyzed conchiolin protein inhibits melanogenesis through PKA/CREB and MEK/ERK signalling pathways (PMC)
HCP skin whitening – MDPI IJMS full text

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Corn Protein
ingredients.note: Conditioning/film-forming plant protein hydrolysate; CIR finds plant-derived protein hydrolysates safe as used. Corn is a low-concern allergen source.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Corn Starch
ingredients.note: Carbohydrate from hydrolyzed corn starch used as a binder, humectant and texturizing agent. Benign, widely used food-grade cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Elastin
ingredients.note: Animal-derived elastin protein fragments used as a humectant and skin/hair conditioner; CIR-reviewed as safe at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Extensin
ingredients.note: Plant cell-wall glycoprotein hydrolysate (often carrot-derived) used as a skin/hair conditioning film former that reduces transepidermal water loss. CIR-reviewed plant proteins deemed safe at use levels.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Gardenia Florida Extract
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed gardenia (jasmine) flower extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Gardenia Florida Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Enzymatically/acid-hydrolyzed gardenia extract used primarily as an antioxidant and moisturizing skin-conditioning agent. Gentle, broadly compatible botanical derivative.

sources.label: The Hair Growth-Promoting Effect of Gardenia florida Fruit Extract and Its Molecular Regulation (PubMed)
Skin Improvement Effects of Gardeniae fructus Extract in HaCaT Keratinocytes, B16F10 Melanocytes, and CCD-986sk Fibroblast Cells (MDPI)

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Glycosaminoglycans
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed connective-tissue polysaccharides used as a humectant and skin/hair conditioner. CIR concluded connective-tissue-derived ingredients are safe as used.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Hyaluronic Acid
ingredients.note: Smaller hyaluronic acid fragments that hydrate and penetrate more readily; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Jojoba Esters
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed jojoba-derived wax esters used as an emollient and skin/hair conditioning agent. Standard, benign jojoba derivative.

sources.label: Evaluation of additive effects of hydrolyzed jojoba esters and glycerol: a preliminary study (PubMed)
Hydrolyzed Jojoba Esters ingredient page – CIR

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Keratin
ingredients.note: Keratin-derived conditioning/antistatic protein used at up to ~5% in hair products; CIR concluded keratin-derived ingredients are safe as used (GMP advised to limit heavy-metal/pesticide impurities).

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Lagenaria Siceraria Fruit Juice
ingredients.note: Enzyme-hydrolyzed bottle gourd (calabash) sap used as a topical skin-conditioning agent. Reported gourd toxicity concerns relate to oral ingestion, not topical cosmetic use.

sources.label: Lagenaria siceraria fruit: A review of its phytochemistry, pharmacology, and promising traditional uses (PubMed)
Lagenaria siceraria phytochemistry and pharmacology review (PMC)

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Linseed Extract
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed flax (linseed) extract rich in polysaccharides used as a film-forming, smoothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Lupine Protein
ingredients.note: Plant (white lupin) protein hydrolysate used as a skin/hair conditioning, firming and moisture-retaining agent. CIR-reviewed plant proteins safe; legume-allergic users should patch test.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Malt Extract
ingredients.note: Hydrolysate of malt extract used as a skin-conditioning agent; standard benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Pea Protein
ingredients.note: Conditioning/film-forming legume protein hydrolysate; CIR reviews plant protein hydrolysates as safe as used, with low irritation/sensitization in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Rice Protein
ingredients.note: Water-soluble rice-derived protein hydrolysate used as antistatic, hair- and skin-conditioning film former and moisturizer.

sources.label: Scientific Literature Review – Hydrolyzed Source Proteins as Used in Cosmetics (CIR PDF)
Safety Assessment of Plant-Derived Proteins and Peptides as Used in Cosmetics (CIR PDF)
Anti-aging efficacy of topical formulations containing niosomes entrapped with rice bran bioactive compounds (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Royal Jelly Protein
ingredients.note: Protein hydrolysate of bee royal jelly used as skin- and hair-conditioning agent. Standard cosmetic protein; not on EWG restricted lists.

sources.label: Antioxidant and Anti-Inflammatory Properties of Hydrolyzed Royal Jelly Peptide in Human Dermal Fibroblasts (PubMed)
Hydrolyzed Royal Jelly Peptide antioxidant/anti-inflammatory – PMC full text
Bioactive Compound Contents and Protective Effects of Royal Jelly Protein Hydrolysates against DNA Oxidative Damage (PMC)

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Sclerotium Gum
ingredients.note: Fermentation-derived polysaccharide (from Sclerotium rolfsii) used as a natural thickener/gelling and stabilizing agent. CIR considers sclerotium gum safe as used.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Microbial Polysaccharide Gums as Used in Cosmetics (PubMed)
Tentative Report Safety Assessment of Microbial Polysaccharide Gums – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Silk
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed silk protein used as a skin and hair conditioning/film-forming agent. Standard benign cosmetic protein.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Sodium Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Low-molecular-weight hyaluronate humectant for deeper hydration; CIR concluded hyaluronates safe as used.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Soy Protein
ingredients.note: Plant-derived protein hydrolysate used as skin- and hair-conditioning agent. CIR found it safe and not a dermal sensitizer up to 20%.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Vegetable Protein
ingredients.note: Plant protein hydrolysate used as a skin and hair conditioning agent. CIR concluded plant-derived proteins/peptides are safe as used; no adverse effects reported in literature.

ingredients.name: Hydrolyzed Viola Tricolor Extract
ingredients.note: Hydrolyzed wild pansy (heartsease) extract used as a soothing/conditioning botanical. Standard plant-derived ingredient with no notable safety concern.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyacetophenone
ingredients.note: Functions as antioxidant, soothing agent and preservative-booster; CIR/FEMA judged it safe and it is GRAS.

ingredients.name: Hydroxycinnamic Acid
ingredients.note: Phenolic acid (coumaric acid family) used as an antioxidant with anti-aging and UV-protective activity. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyethyl Acrylate/Sodium Acryloyldimethyl Taurate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a thickener/emulsion stabilizer; high-MW polymer, not skin-penetrating.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyethyl Urea
ingredients.note: A low-molecular-weight urea derivative used as a humectant; CIR concluded it is safe when formulated to be non-irritating. Standard moisturizing ingredient.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyethylcellulose
ingredients.note: Modified cellulose thickener for clear gels and serums. Inert, non-irritating and widely used.

ingredients.name: Hydroxyphenyl Propamidobenzoic Acid
ingredients.note: Synthetic avenanthramide analogue used as an anti-itch/anti-redness soothing active. A case of allergic contact dermatitis has been reported.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Bislauramide MEA
ingredients.note: Fatty-amide derivative used as a foam booster/viscosity agent and skin conditioner in cleansers; standard mild surfactant adjunct.

sources.label: Final Amended Report on the Safety Assessment of Ethanolamides – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Bispalmitamide MEA
ingredients.note: Synthetic pseudo-ceramide used as a skin barrier and conditioning agent. Standard low-hazard ceramide analog.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Cyclodextrin
ingredients.note: Starch-derived cyclic oligosaccharide used to encapsulate/stabilize actives and act as a chelator/solubilizer; well-characterized and benign.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Guar
ingredients.note: Modified guar gum used as a thickener and skin/hair conditioner. Safe and mild.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Methylcellulose
ingredients.note: Cellulose ether used as a thickener and film former. Safe, food-grade and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyl Starch Phosphate
ingredients.note: Modified starch giving a soft, powdery feel and mild thickening. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Hydroxypropyltrimonium Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Cationic hyaluronate derivative with enhanced substantivity to skin/hair; CIR concluded hyaluronates safe as used.

ingredients.name: Hydroxystearic Acid
ingredients.note: Hydroxylated fatty acid used as an emollient, viscosity/texture agent and emulsion stabilizer. Standard fatty-acid material with low irritation potential.

ingredients.name: Hyssopus Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Hyssop plant extract used as a soothing/conditioning botanical. Standard plant extract with no significant topical safety concern at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Ilex Aquifolium (Holly) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: European holly leaf extract used as a soothing skin conditioner; contains flavonoids and tannins. CAS 90045-83-3, EINECS 289-948-6.

sources.label: Composition and Antimicrobial Activity of Ilex Leaves Water Extracts (PubMed)
Ilex aquifolium: protection against enzymatic and non-enzymatic lipid peroxidation (PubMed)
Composition and Antimicrobial Activity of Ilex Leaves Water Extracts (PMC)

ingredients.name: Ilex Guayusa Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Caffeine-containing holly leaf extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Ilex Paraguariensis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Yerba mate leaf extract (polyphenol/caffeine source) used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract.

ingredients.name: Illite
ingredients.note: Natural clay mineral used as an absorbent, abrasive and bulking agent. Benign inorganic ingredient (CAS 12173-60-3).

sources.label: Natural Illite Liquid Mineral Extract: A Clinical Study of an Emulsion to Improve Skin Barrier Function (MDPI)
Natural and ion-exchanged illite clays reduce bacterial burden and inflammation in cutaneous MRSA infections in mice (PubMed)
Amended Safety Assessment of Naturally-Sourced Clays as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Indigofera Tinctoria Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: True indigo leaf extract (CAS 84775-63-3) used as a non-oxidative hair colorant; SCCS has assessed it as a hair dye. Standard plant-based hair coloring agent.

ingredients.name: Inulin
ingredients.note: Plant polysaccharide used as a hydrating prebiotic; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Iron Oxides
ingredients.note: Inert mineral pigments (red, yellow, black) approved by FDA and EU for use including eye area. Generally regarded as safe and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Isoamyl Laurate
ingredients.note: An ester of lauric acid and isoamyl alcohol used as a lightweight, dry-feel emollient and silicone alternative. Benign cosmetic emollient.

ingredients.name: Isobutane
ingredients.note: Common aerosol propellant that is low concern dermally but extremely flammable and poses an inhalation hazard. Precautionary for safety hazards rather than toxicity.

ingredients.name: Isodecyl Isononanoate
ingredients.note: Branched-chain ester used as a lightweight emollient and antistatic skin-conditioning agent. Covered by CIR alkyl esters review as safe; low comedogenicity.

ingredients.name: Isodecyl Neopentanoate
ingredients.note: Branched-chain synthetic ester used as a light, dry-feel emollient. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic emollient.

ingredients.name: Isododecane
ingredients.note: Volatile, lightweight hydrocarbon used for a dry, fast-spreading feel; inert and safe on skin.

ingredients.name: Isohexadecane
ingredients.note: Light emollient hydrocarbon giving smooth slip; benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Isoleucine
ingredients.note: Branched-chain amino acid used as a skin-conditioning and antistatic agent. Benign topical cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Isononyl Isononanoate
ingredients.note: Branched-chain ester emollient giving a dry, silky skin feel. CIR assessed alkyl esters of this type as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Isopentyldiol
ingredients.note: Diol used as a solvent, humectant and viscosity-control agent with mild antimicrobial boosting. Reviewed as a safe cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Volatile solvent and astringent; low concern in cosmetic use but drying and potentially irritating on skin, and flammable. Precautionary for its drying effect rather than toxicity.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Myristate
ingredients.note: Lightweight spreading ester; safe but moderately comedogenic, so acne-prone users may prefer to limit it.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Palmitate
ingredients.note: Smooth-feel emollient ester; safe but can be comedogenic for some skin.

ingredients.name: Isopropyl Titanium Triisostearate
ingredients.note: Titanium-based surface coating that binds and disperses pigments and TiO2; CIR concluded organo-titanium ingredient safe as used.

ingredients.name: Isosorbide Dicaprylate
ingredients.note: Diester of isosorbide and caprylic acid used as a lightweight emollient and skin-conditioning agent (CAS 64896-70-4). REACH registered in the EU; standard benign ingredient.

sources.label: Isosorbide Fatty Acid Diesters Have Synergistic Anti-Inflammatory Effects in Atopic Dermatitis Models (PubMed)
Isosorbide Diesters: Mechanistic Insights and Therapeutic Applications in Skin and Neuroinflammatory Disorders (PMC)
Isosorbide Diesters – doi.org IJMS full text

ingredients.name: Isostearic Acid
ingredients.note: A branched C18 fatty acid used as an emollient and emulsion stabilizer; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Isostearyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Branched-chain fatty alcohol used as an emollient. CIR Expert Panel concluded the fatty alcohols including isostearyl alcohol are safe; non-irritating and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Isostearyl Isostearate
ingredients.note: Branched fatty-acid ester used as an emollient and skin-conditioning agent. Standard benign cosmetic emollient.

ingredients.name: Isostearyl Neopentanoate
ingredients.note: Branched fatty-alcohol ester of neopentanoic acid used as a light, silky emollient and spreading agent. Standard synthetic ester, low irritation.

ingredients.name: Isotridecyl Isononanoate
ingredients.note: Branched-chain fatty-alcohol/fatty-acid ester used as a light, non-greasy emollient and spreading agent. Standard synthetic ester, low irritation.

ingredients.name: Jania Rubens Extract
ingredients.note: Calcified red algae extract used as an antioxidant/soothing skin-conditioning botanical; non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Jasminum Grandiflorum (Jasmine) Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Jasmine stem (non-flower) extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical; unlike the fragrant flower absolute it is not the regulated EU fragrance-allergen part.

sources.label: Efficacy of Jasminum grandiflorum L. leaf extract on dermal wound healing in rats (PubMed)
Effects of ethanolic extract of Jasminum grandiflorum flowers on wound healing in diabetic rats (PMC)
Jasminum sambac Cell Extract as Antioxidant Booster against Skin Aging (PMC)

ingredients.name: Jojoba Esters
ingredients.note: Jojoba-derived wax esters used as emollients and texture enhancers mimicking skin lipids. CIR found jojoba esters non-irritating and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Jojoba Oil
ingredients.note: Liquid wax ester closely mimicking skin sebum; stable, well-tolerated emollient with very low irritancy.

ingredients.name: Juniperus Mexicana Oil
ingredients.note: Texas cedarwood essential oil used as a fragrance agent. Not a prohibited substance and reported free of the EU 26 named allergens; dilute and patch test, as essential oils can irritate sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Kaolin
ingredients.note: Naturally occurring clay used to absorb oil and add slip; regarded as inert and safe in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Kappaphycus Alvarezii Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of an edible red seaweed used as a conditioning/film-forming agent. Considered a benign cosmetic botanical.

ingredients.name: Kjellmaniella Gyrata Extract
ingredients.note: Fucoidan-rich brown macroalgae extract used for skin/hair conditioning; benign seaweed extract.

ingredients.name: Kummerowia Striata Extract
ingredients.note: Extract of Kummerowia striata (Japanese clover) used as a soothing, antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign botanical ingredient.

ingredients.name: Lactic Acid
ingredients.note: Alpha hydroxy acid exfoliant safe at cosmetic levels. Increases sun sensitivity and can over-dry or irritate at high concentrations/low pH.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus
ingredients.note: Probiotic bacterial genus or its lysate used as a skin-conditioning/microbiome-supporting agent. No notable topical safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus Ferment
ingredients.note: Postbiotic fermentation product (metabolites, not live bacteria) used as a skin-conditioning/microbiome agent; CIR found it safe.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Centella Asiatica Extract Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica extract fermented by Lactobacillus, used as a soothing/barrier-supporting skin-conditioning agent. Benign fermented botanical.

sources.label: Lactobacillus-Fermented Centella asiatica Extract Inhibits Airway Inflammation (PubMed)
Moisturizing and Antiinflammatory Properties of Cosmetic Formulations Containing Centella asiatica Extract (PubMed)
Galactomyces Ferment Filtrate, Dexpanthenol, and Centella Asiatica Serum for Post-Acne Hyperpigmentation (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Hibiscus Sabdariffa Flower Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Probiotic ferment filtrate of hibiscus flower used as a skin-conditioning/antioxidant active; benign cosmetic ferment.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Kelp Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Kelp fermented with Lactobacillus used as a humectant/skin-conditioning bioferment delivering polysaccharides and amino acids; benign.

sources.label: Bioferments and Biosurfactants as New Products with Potential Use in the Cosmetic Industry (MDPI)
Unlocking the Potential of Fermentation in Cosmetics: A Review (MDPI)
Regulatory effects of Lactobacillus plantarum-GMNL6 on human skin health by improving skin microbiome (PMC)

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Rice Ferment
ingredients.note: Probiotic ferment of rice by Lactobacillus used as a soothing/conditioning active. Common benign biotech cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Lactobacillus/Soymilk Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Soymilk fermented with Lactobacillus used as a skin-conditioning ferment; benign, low-hazard.

ingredients.name: Lactobionic Acid
ingredients.note: Polyhydroxy acid exfoliant that is gentle and humectant with less sun-sensitivity than AHAs. Mild irritation possible at high concentration.

ingredients.name: Lactococcus Ferment Lysate
ingredients.note: Probiotic-derived lysate of Lactococcus lactis used to support barrier repair, microbiome and soothing for sensitive skin. Gentle and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Lactose
ingredients.note: Disaccharide sugar used as a humectant/skin-conditioning agent. Benign standard cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Laminaria Digitata
ingredients.note: Brown kelp (seaweed) used as a skin-protecting/conditioning botanical; benign, though naturally iodine-containing.

ingredients.name: Laminaria Digitata Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (kelp) extract rich in polysaccharides, vitamins and minerals; used as antioxidant, soothing and skin-protecting agent. Minimal allergenic potential.

sources.label: A novel topical ingredient derived from seaweed significantly reduces symptoms of acne vulgaris (PubMed)
Effect of a brown seaweed (Laminaria digitata) extract on skin (PubMed)
Potential Use of Seaweed Bioactive Compounds in Skincare—A Review (PMC)

ingredients.name: Laminaria Japonica Extract
ingredients.note: Brown seaweed (kelp) extract used as a humectant/skin-conditioning botanical; a standard benign marine extract.

sources.label: Effects of seaweed Laminaria japonica extracts on skin moisturizing activity in vivo (PubMed)
Inhibition of UVB-Induced Inflammation by Laminaria japonica Extract via Regulation of nc886-PKR Pathway (PMC)
Chemical Composition and Antioxidant and Antibacterial Activities of an Essential Oil from Laminaria japonica (PMC)

ingredients.name: Lanthionine
ingredients.note: Cross-link amino acid formed in hair during lye/hydroxide relaxing; not added as an ingredient and considered low concern itself.

ingredients.name: Lauramidopropyl Betaine
ingredients.note: Amphoteric surfactant/foam booster; CIR found alkyl betaines safe when formulated to be non-sensitizing (DMAPA/amidoamine impurities controlled to low ppm).

ingredients.name: Laureth-21
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated lauryl alcohol used as a surfactant/solubilizer; CIR found Laureths safe when formulated non-irritating. Trace 1,4-dioxane controlled in manufacturing.

sources.label: Final report of the amended safety assessment of sodium laureth sulfate and related salts of sulfated ethoxylated alcohols (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Laureth-23
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated lauryl alcohol (23 EO) used as a nonionic surfactant, emulsifier and solubilizer. CIR concluded laureths are safe; PEG ingredients should use suitably purified grades.

ingredients.name: Lauric Acid
ingredients.note: A C12 saturated fatty acid used as a surfactant and emulsifier; reviewed as safe by CIR. Can be comedogenic but not a toxicity concern.

ingredients.name: Lauroyl Lysine
ingredients.note: Amino-acid-derived powder used to improve adhesion and silky feel in powders; mild and considered safe.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Betaine
ingredients.note: Amphoteric/zwitterionic surfactant used to cleanse, boost foam and reduce harshness of anionic detergents. CIR found safe when formulated non-irritating.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Alkyl Betaines as Used in Cosmetics – CIR Final Report (PDF)
Safety assessment of cocamidopropyl betaine, a cosmetic ingredient (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Lauryl Dimethicone/Polyglycerin-3 Crosspolymer
ingredients.note: Silicone-polyglycerin crosspolymer used as a water-in-oil emulsifier and surfactant. Standard formulation aid used at low levels.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Dimethicone Crosspolymers as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Lauryl Glucoside
ingredients.note: Mild biodegradable glucoside surfactant from lauryl alcohol and glucose, used in low-irritation cleansers. Considered safe with minimal sensitization.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Hydroxysultaine
ingredients.note: Mild amphoteric foam booster and viscosity builder used as a gentler alternative to betaines. Low irritation and good skin tolerance.

ingredients.name: Lauryl Laurate
ingredients.note: Wax-like ester of lauryl alcohol and lauric acid used as an emollient and texture agent. CIR deemed it safe for cosmetic use (assessed up to 16%).

ingredients.name: Lauryl PEG-10 Tris(Trimethylsiloxy)Silylethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A silicone-based nonionic emulsifier used to stabilize water-in-silicone emulsions. Considered a benign, inert formulation aid.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Lauryl PEG-8 Dimethicone
ingredients.note: PEG-modified silicone used as a water-in-oil emulsifier and conditioning agent. CIR considers dimethicone copolymers safe as used.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Polyoxyalkylene Siloxane Copolymers as Used in Cosmetics – CIR Final Report (PDF)

ingredients.name: Lauryl PEG-9 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-based nonionic emulsifier used to stabilize water-in-silicone emulsions (common in color cosmetics/sunscreens). Inert, low irritation.

ingredients.name: Lauryl PEG/PPG-18/18 Methicone
ingredients.note: PEG/PPG-modified silicone (alkyl dimethicone copolyol) used as a water-in-silicone emulsifier giving lightweight, stable emulsions. Benign cosmetic ingredient.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Alkoxy Polysiloxanes as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)

ingredients.name: Lauryl Polyglyceryl-3 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: A polyglyceryl-modified silicone surfactant used as a W/O (water-in-silicone) emulsifier. Benign formulation/texture agent.

ingredients.name: Lecithin
ingredients.note: Natural phospholipid emulsifier from soy or sunflower. Safe and skin-conditioning; useful in liposomal delivery.

ingredients.name: Leontopodium Alpinum Callus Culture Extract
ingredients.note: Edelweiss plant cell culture extract used as an antioxidant/soothing botanical. Good safety profile and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Leptospermum Scoparium Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Manuka leaf extract used as an antimicrobial, anti-inflammatory and antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated in cosmetic use.

sources.label: Investigations of kanuka and manuka essential oils for in vitro treatment of disease and cellular inflammation (PubMed)
Mānuka Oil—A Review of Antimicrobial and Other Medicinal Properties (PMC)
TLC-Bioautography-Guided Isolation of Antibacterial Compounds from Manuka (Leptospermum scoparium) Leaf and Branch Extracts (PMC)

ingredients.name: Leucine
ingredients.note: Branched-chain amino acid used as a skin-conditioning/humectant agent and anti-caking aid. Benign standard ingredient.

ingredients.name: Leuconostoc/Radish Root Ferment Filtrate
ingredients.note: Natural ferment-derived antimicrobial peptide (bacteriocin) used as an alternative preservative; low-hazard and well tolerated.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Radish Root-Derived Ingredients as Used in Cosmetics – CIR (PDF)
Identification of didecyldimethylammonium salts and salicylic acid as antimicrobial compounds in commercial fermented radish kimchi (PubMed)

ingredients.name: Levulinic Acid
ingredients.note: Plant-derived organic acid used as a preservative-booster, pH adjuster and skin conditioner. CIR concluded it is safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Licorice Root Extract
ingredients.note: Glabridin and glycyrrhizinates calm redness and brighten uneven tone; well tolerated and a staple anti-inflammatory botanical.

ingredients.name: Limnanthes Alba (Meadowfoam) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Oxidatively stable, long-chain-ester-rich seed oil used as an emollient and skin conditioner. Standard low-hazard plant oil.

ingredients.name: Linoleic Acid
ingredients.note: Essential fatty acid that supports skin barrier; safe and beneficial emollient.

ingredients.name: Linolenic Acid
ingredients.note: Omega-3 essential fatty acid used as skin-conditioning/barrier-supporting agent with anti-inflammatory properties.

sources.label: Alpha-linolenic acid modulates skin barrier lipidome - PubMed
Therapeutic Benefits of Topical Omega-3 Polyunsaturated Fatty Acids in Skin Diseases - PMC

ingredients.name: Linum Usitatissimum (Linseed) Seed Extract
ingredients.note: Flax/linseed-derived plant extract used as a skin-conditioning agent. Benign standard botanical.

ingredients.name: Linum Usitatissimum (Linseed) Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Flax/linseed oil rich in omega-3 fatty acids, used as a conditioning emollient. Benign botanical oil.

ingredients.name: Liriope Muscari Root Extract
ingredients.note: Lilyturf root extract used as a skin-conditioning/softening botanical. Standard, low-concern cosmetic plant ingredient.

ingredients.name: Lonicera Japonica (Honeysuckle) Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Honeysuckle leaf extract with antibacterial/anti-inflammatory constituents, sometimes used as a natural preservative-booster. Contains naturally occurring p-hydroxybenzoic acid (not parabens).

sources.label: Lonicera japonica: Ethnopharmacology, phytochemistry and pharmacology - PMC
Wound repair and anti-inflammatory potential of Lonicera japonica - PMC
Honeysuckle flower-loaded nanoemulsions for skin health - doi.org

ingredients.name: Luffa Cylindrica Fruit
ingredients.note: Loofah (sponge gourd) fruit used as a botanical skin-conditioning and mild physical exfoliating ingredient. Not known to be comedogenic or sensitizing.

sources.label: Antioxidant constituents in the fruits of Luffa cylindrica - PubMed
Phytochemical Studies and Antioxidant Potential of Luffa cylindrica Fruit - PubMed
Luffa cylindrica suppresses atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions - PMC

ingredients.name: Luffa Cylindrica Fruit/Leaf/Stem Extract
ingredients.note: Loofah plant extract used as a hydrating, soothing, antioxidant skin conditioner. Generally non-irritating; patch test advised for sensitive skin.

sources.label: Antioxidant constituents in the fruits of Luffa cylindrica - PubMed
Luffa cylindrica suppresses atopic dermatitis-like skin lesions - PMC
Luffa cylindrica seed oil - CIR Cosmetic Ingredient Review

ingredients.name: Lupinus Albus Seed Extract
ingredients.note: White lupin seed extract used as a soothing, peptide-rich, skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Lutein
ingredients.note: Carotenoid antioxidant from marigold supporting skin against blue-light and oxidative stress. Considered safe in topical use.

ingredients.name: Lycopene
ingredients.note: Red carotenoid antioxidant from tomatoes that quenches singlet oxygen. Safe and non-sensitizing at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Lysine
ingredients.note: An essential amino acid used as a skin/hair conditioning and humectant agent. Benign, naturally occurring ingredient.

ingredients.name: Lysolecithin
ingredients.note: A lysophospholipid derived from natural lecithin, used as a co-emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent at low levels. Benign, naturally derived texture aid.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Lecithin and Other Phosphoglycerides as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
Safety Assessment of Lecithin and Other Phosphoglycerides - PubMed

ingredients.name: Macadamia Integrifolia Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Plant seed oil rich in oleic and palmitoleic acids, used as an emollient skin conditioner. Permitted under EU 1223/2009.

sources.label: Macadamia integrifolia seed oil - CIR Cosmetic Ingredient Review
Clinical Efficacy of Nanoemulsions Containing Macadamia integrifolia Seed Oil - PMC
Clinical Efficacy of Nanoemulsions Containing Macadamia integrifolia Seed Oil - doi.org

ingredients.name: Macadamia Seed Oil Polyglyceryl-6 Esters Behenate
ingredients.note: Macadamia-oil-derived polyglyceryl ester acting as an emollient, skin conditioner and mild surfactant. Tested negative in Ames assay; benign.

ingredients.name: Macadamia Ternifolia Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Emollient oil pressed from macadamia nuts, rich in oleic and palmitoleic acids; reviewed as safe by CIR. Standard benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.name: Madecassic Acid
ingredients.note: Centella asiatica triterpene with soothing/anti-inflammatory and collagen-supporting activity; CIR found Centella-derived ingredients safe.

ingredients.name: Madecassoside
ingredients.note: Soothing antioxidant from centella; safe.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Aluminum Silicate
ingredients.note: Smectite clay mineral used as a thickener, emulsion stabilizer and viscosity controller. CIR concluded it safe as used; non-irritating/non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Ascorbyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: Stable vitamin C derivative that brightens and supports collagen with low irritation. Safe brightener at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Aspartate
ingredients.note: Magnesium salt of aspartic acid used as a hydrating/softening skin-conditioning agent. CIR found alpha-amino acids safe as used.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Chloride
ingredients.note: Inorganic salt used as a viscosity controller and electrolyte; recognized as safe and ubiquitous.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Gluconate
ingredients.note: Magnesium salt of gluconic acid used as a skin-conditioning and buffering agent. Benign mineral-salt cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Myristate
ingredients.note: Fatty-acid magnesium salt used as a slip and adhesion agent in pressed powders; inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Oxide
ingredients.note: Inorganic magnesium mineral used as an absorbent, opacifier and texture/bulking agent; related inorganic hydroxides are GRAS. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Stearate
ingredients.note: Common metallic-soap binder providing adhesion and slip in pressed powders; inert and generally regarded as safe.

ingredients.name: Magnesium Sulfate
ingredients.note: Inorganic salt used as a viscosity/bulking agent and water-in-oil emulsion stabilizer. FDA-recognized and low hazard at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Magnesium/Potassium/Silicon/Fluoride/Hydroxide/Oxide
ingredients.note: Synthetic mica/talc-derived mineral (made by heating talc with potassium silicofluoride) used as a bulking agent/substrate in pressed powders and makeup.

sources.label: PubChem: Magnesium potassium silicon fluoride hydroxide oxide
CIR Tentative Safety Assessment: Synthetic Fluorphlogopite (synthetic mica, Mg/K/Si/F/OH mineral) as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Magnolia Kobus Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Magnolia bark extract used as an antioxidant/soothing botanical (magnolol/honokiol content). Benign standard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Malachite Extract
ingredients.note: Copper-rich extract of malachite mineral used as an antioxidant (copper cofactor for SOD). Well tolerated at cosmetic levels with no EU Annex II/III restrictions.

Metals in cosmetics: implications for human health - PubMed

ingredients.name: Malic Acid
ingredients.note: Alpha hydroxy acid often paired with other acids for exfoliation and pH adjustment. Increases sun sensitivity and may irritate at high concentration/low pH.

ingredients.name: Malpighia Punicifolia (Acerola) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Acerola (Barbados cherry) fruit extract, naturally vitamin-C-rich, used as an antioxidant skin conditioner. Generally well tolerated.

Protective effect of acerola against oxidative damage in human dermal fibroblasts - PubMed

ingredients.name: Malt Extract
ingredients.note: Aqueous extract of germinated barley used as skin-conditioning, moisturizing emollient/tonic with antioxidant properties.

ingredients.name: Maltodextrin
ingredients.note: Starch-derived polysaccharide used as a binder, film former and carrier. CIR and FDA recognize it as safe.

ingredients.name: Malva Sylvestris (Mallow) Extract
ingredients.note: Mucilage-rich mallow plant extract used as a soothing skin-conditioning agent. No significant safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Malva Sylvestris (Mallow) Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Mallow extract rich in mucilage polysaccharides and tannins used as a soothing, hydrating, anti-inflammatory botanical.

ingredients.name: Mandelic Acid
ingredients.note: Large-molecule AHA derived from bitter almonds, gentler than glycolic acid. Still increases UV sensitivity and may irritate at high strength.

ingredients.name: Manganese Violet
ingredients.note: Insoluble manganese ammonium pyrophosphate pigment. Approved by FDA and EU for general cosmetic use; considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Mangifera Indica (Mango) Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Antioxidant-rich mango fruit extract used as a skin-conditioning agent. Considered safe for topical use.

ingredients.name: Mangifera Indica (Mango) Pulp Extract
ingredients.note: Mango fruit pulp extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign food-derived extract.

ingredients.name: Mangifera Indica (Mango) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Fat from mango seeds rich in fatty acids and antioxidants, used as an emollient and barrier-forming butter. CAS 90063-86-8, EINECS 290-045-4.

ingredients.name: Mango Butter
ingredients.note: Soft occlusive butter; safe and well-tolerated emollient.

ingredients.name: Mannitol
ingredients.note: Sugar alcohol used as a humectant and texture agent. Well-established low-hazard cosmetic ingredient, also FDA-recognized.

ingredients.name: Mannose
ingredients.note: Monosaccharide (glucose epimer) used as a humectant and skin-conditioning agent; reviewed by CIR within monosaccharides/disaccharides as safe. Benign sugar naturally present in the body.

ingredients.name: Medicago Sativa (Alfalfa) Extract
ingredients.note: Whole-plant alfalfa extract (Fabaceae) used as a toning/skin-conditioning botanical with antioxidant activity. EINECS 281-984-0.

ingredients.name: Melia Azadirachta Bark Extract
ingredients.note: Neem bark extract used as a soothing, antimicrobial, skin-conditioning botanical. Generally well tolerated in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Melia Azadirachta Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Neem flower botanical used as a soothing antioxidant skin-conditioning agent; low irritation/sensitization risk at use levels.

Bioactive Azadirachta indica and Melia azedarach leaves extracts - PMC

ingredients.name: Melia Azadirachta Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Neem leaf botanical valued for soothing, antioxidant and antimicrobial conditioning; low irritation/sensitization risk at use levels.

sources.label: Bioactive Azadirachta indica and Melia azedarach leaves extracts - PMC
Bioactive Azadirachta indica and Melia azedarach leaves extracts - PubMed

ingredients.name: Melia Azadirachta Leaf/Flower Extract
ingredients.note: Neem leaf/flower extract with antibacterial and anti-inflammatory bioactives (nimbidin, nimbin) used in acne/sensitive-skin formulas. Low irritation/sensitization risk at typical levels.

sources.label: Bioactive Azadirachta indica and Melia azedarach leaves extracts - PMC
Effect of cream containing Melia azedarach flowers on skin - PubMed

ingredients.name: Melissa Officinalis Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon balm extract used as an antioxidant/skin-conditioning botanical with a long history of safe use. Aromatic components may irritate sensitive skin at high levels; non-genotoxic.

ingredients.name: Melissa Officinalis Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Lemon balm (Lamiaceae) leaf extract used as an antioxidant, soothing skin conditioner. Generally well tolerated as a low-level extract.

ingredients.name: Mentha Aquatica Extract
ingredients.note: Water mint extract used as an antioxidant/soothing skin-conditioning botanical with a light cooling effect. Lower allergen load than mint essential oils; patch test advised for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Mentha Suaveolens Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Apple mint leaf extract used as an astringent, soothing skin-conditioning botanical. Benign plant extract at typical low levels.

Anti-Thermal Skin Aging Activity of Apple Mint (Mentha suaveolens) in Human Dermal Fibroblasts - PMC

ingredients.name: Mentha Viridis (Spearmint) Extract
ingredients.note: Spearmint leaf extract used as a refreshing skin-conditioning botanical; as an extract (vs essential oil) sensitization risk is low. Standard botanical at typical use levels.

ingredients.name: Menthoxypropanediol
ingredients.note: Synthetic menthol derivative used as a long-lasting cooling/sensate agent in skin, lip, hair and shaving products.

Anti-itching effects of menthoxypropanediol (TRPM8 agonist) cream in atopic dermatitis - PubMed

ingredients.name: Menthyl Lactate
ingredients.note: A long-lasting cooling/sensate agent (menthol-lactic acid ester) milder than menthol; reported non-genotoxic, non-sensitizing and non-irritating at typical 0.1-1% use.

ingredients.name: Methicone
ingredients.note: Methyl hydrogen silicone used as a hydrophobic surface coating for pigments and a skin conditioner; CIR-assessed as safe when non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Methionine
ingredients.note: Essential sulfur-containing amino acid used as an antioxidant and conditioning agent. Considered safe in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Methyl Diisopropyl Propionamide
ingredients.note: Synthetic menthol-free cooling/sensate agent (WS-23) with long-lasting, low-odor cooling effect. Considered non-irritating and non-sensitizing at use levels.

ingredients.name: Methyl Gluceth-20
ingredients.note: Glucose-derived PEG humectant used to bind water and soften skin. CIR considers methyl glucose ethers safe as used.

ingredients.name: Methyl Glucose Sesquistearate
ingredients.note: Sugar-derived (methyl glucose + stearic acid) nonionic emulsifier and skin softener. CIR found it safe; non-irritating even at high levels.

sources.label: Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters as Used in Cosmetics - CIR
CIR Safety Assessment of Methyl Glucose Polyethers and Esters (PDF)

ingredients.name: Methyl Trimethicone
ingredients.note: Volatile silicone used as a light, fast-spreading emollient and carrier. Standard low-hazard silicone fluid.

ingredients.name: Methylene Bis-Benzotriazolyl Tetramethylbutylphenol
ingredients.note: Broad-spectrum organic particulate filter (Tinosorb M) that works partly by scattering, with a large insoluble molecule and minimal absorption. Photostable and very well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Methylglucamine
ingredients.note: An organic amine base used as a pH-adjusting and solubilizing agent; widely used as a pharmaceutical excipient and generally regarded as nontoxic at use levels.

ingredients.name: Methylpropanediol
ingredients.note: Humectant and penetration aid; CIR concluded it is safe at use levels with no notable irritation or sensitization.

ingredients.name: Mica
ingredients.note: Natural silicate mineral used as a pearlescent base and filler. Considered safe topically; ethical-sourcing concerns are separate from skin safety.

ingredients.name: Micrococcus Lysate
ingredients.note: Bacterial lysate (enzymes, peptides) used as a skin-conditioning/DNA-repair-support active; CosIng function skin conditioning.

ingredients.name: Microcrystalline Cellulose
ingredients.note: Purified cellulose used as a thickener, bulking agent and stabilizer. Inert, widely used and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Microcrystalline Wax
ingredients.note: Petroleum-derived structuring wax used in balms and sticks; inert and safe.

ingredients.name: Milk Protein Extract
ingredients.note: Amino-acid and peptide-rich milk-derived protein used as a film-forming, moisturizing and skin-conditioning agent. CIR-reviewed milk proteins are safe; rare reactions in dairy-allergic users.

ingredients.name: Mineral Oil
ingredients.note: Highly refined cosmetic-grade mineral oil is inert and non-comedogenic in most formulas; regulators consider refined grades safe, unlike crude/unrefined oils.

ingredients.name: Mineral Salt
ingredients.note: Generic mineral salt blend used as a viscosity/texture agent and astringent. Benign inorganic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Mineral Salts
ingredients.note: Generic mix of inorganic mineral salts used for conditioning, viscosity or fragrance-masking. Generally benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Momordica Charantia Fruit Extract
ingredients.note: Bitter melon fruit extract with antioxidant, antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory compounds used for oily/acne-prone skin. Standard benign cosmetic plant extract at 1-3%.

ingredients.note: Noni flower extract rich in flavonoids and phenolics, used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. Benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Morinda Citrifolia Leaf Extract
ingredients.note: Noni leaf extract containing flavonoids, vitamins and antioxidants. Used as a skin-conditioning botanical; benign at cosmetic levels.

ingredients.name: Moringa Oleifera Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Emollient oil pressed from moringa seeds, rich in oleic acid. Standard benign cosmetic oil.

ingredients.note: Banana fruit pulp extract used as a humectant/skin-conditioning botanical. Benign food-derived extract.

ingredients.note: Forget-me-not (Boraginaceae) aerial-part extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Standard, low-concern cosmetic plant extract.

ingredients.name: Myristyl Glucoside

ingredients.name: Nylon-6

ingredients.name: Nylon-66

ingredients.name: Nymphaea Caerulea Flower Extract

ingredients.name: Octisalate

ingredients.name: Octyldodecanol

ingredients.name: Octyldodeceth-16

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Myristate
ingredients.note: Ester of octyldodecanol and myristic acid used as a low-irritation emollient; CIR concluded myristate esters safe.

ingredients.name: Octyldodecyl Neopentanoate
ingredients.note: Light viscous ester emollient providing glide, reduced tack and pigment dispersion. EWG low-hazard (1), no significant irritation or sensitization concerns.

ingredients.name: Oryza Sativa (Rice) Bran Extract

ingredients.name: Pisum Sativum (Pea) Extract
ingredients.note: Pea-derived extract used as a skin-conditioning botanical. Benign in cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Platonia Insignis (Bacuri) Seed Butter
ingredients.note: Amazonian bacuri seed butter used as an emollient/skin-conditioning fat; benign cosmetic plant butter.

ingredients.name: Plukenetia Volubilis Seed Oil
ingredients.note: Omega-rich sacha inchi seed oil used as an emollient/skin-conditioning oil; standard cosmetic plant oil.

ingredients.name: Poly C10-30 Alkyl Acrylate
ingredients.note: Crosslinked acrylate polymer used as an emulsion stabilizer, film former and rheology modifier; large polymer with low absorption, CIR-supported.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate Crosspolymer-6
ingredients.note: Electrolyte-tolerant acrylic thickener giving a light gel texture. Considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate-13
ingredients.note: Synthetic acrylate copolymer used as a thickener and emulsion stabilizer (often paired with Polyisobutene). Inert, standard rheology modifier.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylate-21
ingredients.note: Acrylate copolymer used as an emulsion stabilizer, film former and viscosity/rheology modifier; high-molecular-weight polymer reviewed as safe with negligible skin penetration.

ingredients.name: Polyacrylic Acid
ingredients.note: Crosslinked acrylic-acid polymer (carbomer-type) used as a thickener, stabilizer and film former. CIR found acrylate polymers/carbomers safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polybutene
ingredients.note: Tacky liquid polymer used for gloss and adhesion in lip products; considered safe and non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyester-5
ingredients.note: Synthetic copolymer used as a film former and viscosity controller in makeup and hair styling products. No significant safety concern.

ingredients.name: Polyether-1
ingredients.note: PEG/PPG-based copolymer (PEG-180/dodoxynol-5/PEG-25 tristyrylphenol/glycoluril) used as a film former and texture/viscosity agent. Benign.

ingredients.name: Polyglutamic Acid
ingredients.note: Fermentation-derived peptide humectant that holds water at the skin surface; benign and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-10
ingredients.note: Polymerized glycerin used as a humectant and moisturizing agent. Benign, glycerin-class hydrating ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-3
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol humectant and solvent used to hydrate and improve texture. Standard low-hazard cosmetic moisturizing ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglycerin-6
ingredients.note: Oligomer of glycerin used as a humectant, solvent and skin-conditioning agent. Glycerin-family material, well-tolerated and non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Decaisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and dispersant. Glyceryl-ester surfactant, well-tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic PEG-free polyglycerin/isostearic acid ester used as a gentle emulsifier; covered by CIR polyglyceryl fatty acid ester assessment.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Dioleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol diester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Benign texture agent.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Eicosanedioate/Tetradecanedioate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerin diacid esters used as an emollient, skin conditioner and gelling/emulsion agent. Covered by CIR polyglyceryl fatty acid ester assessment; non-irritating in testing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic polyglycerol ester O/W emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Standard texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Laurate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived nonionic surfactant/emulsifier; CIR found polyglyceryl fatty-acid esters safe and it is non-sensitizing.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Mono/Dioleate
ingredients.note: Mixed mono/diester of decaglycerol and oleic acid used as a non-ionic emulsifier/surfactant. Polyglyceryl fatty-acid esters are CIR-reviewed and safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Myristate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and surfactant. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol-ester, non-ionic emulsifier/surfactant used in mild cleansers and oil-in-water emulsions. Standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-10 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl fatty-acid ester used as a nonionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. CIR found polyglyceryl esters safe when formulated to be non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Diisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of isostearic acid used as a nonionic W/O emulsifier; mild and standard.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Dipolyhydroxystearate
ingredients.note: A polyglycerol ester used as a water-in-oil emulsifier. Standard texture/emulsion ingredient with no notable safety concerns.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a mild nonionic (often W/O) emulsifier and emollient. Standard glyceryl-ester surfactant, low irritation.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Isostearate/Dimer Dilinoleate Copolymer
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl-based ester copolymer used as an emulsifier and texturizing/structuring agent. Standard, low-concern cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Laurate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of lauric acid used as a nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Sesquicaprylate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol caprylate ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and emollient. Benign texture ingredient.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Stearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of stearic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier. Standard, well-tolerated emulsifying agent.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-2 Triisostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol-ester, water-in-oil emulsifier and emollient used to stabilize anhydrous and oily formulas. Standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Beeswax
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of beeswax acids used as a non-ionic emulsifier and texture/structuring agent. Benign cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Diisostearate

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Distearate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty-acid ester used as a nonionic oil-in-water emulsifier. CIR considers polyglyceryl esters safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Methylglucose Distearate
ingredients.note: Non-ionic, glucose-derived emulsifier used to stabilize oil-in-water creams. Standard, mild cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of oleic acid used as a non-ionic emulsifier and skin-conditioning agent. Polyglyceryl esters are CIR-reviewed and considered safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polydimethylsiloxyethyl Dimethicone
ingredients.note: Silicone-based polyglyceryl emulsifier used for water-in-silicone systems. Inert, standard cosmetic emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-3 Polyricinoleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of ricinoleic acid used as a water-in-oil emulsifier. CIR considers polyglyceryl esters safe as used.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Caprate
ingredients.note: Plant-derived, PEG-free nonionic O/W emulsifier and solubilizer. Gentle, low-irritation, suitable for sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Isostearate
ingredients.note: Polyglyceryl ester used as a mild non-ionic emulsifier. CIR safety assessment of polyglyceryl fatty acid esters found them safe; non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-4 Laurate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol fatty acid ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier/surfactant. Standard, gentle cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.note: Nonionic polyglycerol-based ether emulsifier used to stabilize oil/water emulsions and improve foam. Standard, well-tolerated emulsifying agent.

ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester used as a mild nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer. CIR-reviewed as safe.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Oleate
ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of oleic acid used as a nonionic emulsifier. Standard benign emulsifier.

ingredients.name: Polyglyceryl-6 Polyricinoleate

ingredients.note: Polyglycerol ester of stearic acid used as a mild nonionic emulsifier. Benign texture/emulsifying ingredient.

ingredients.note: Buckwheat seed extract rich in rutin/flavonoids, used as an antioxidant skin-conditioning botanical. A standard, low-hazard plant extract.

ingredients.name: Polyhydroxystearic Acid
ingredients.note: Fatty-acid-derived dispersant/emulsifier used to coat and stabilize pigments and oils. Standard cosmetic surface treatment.

ingredients.name: Polyisobutene
ingredients.note: Synthetic hydrocarbon polymer used as an emollient and film-former. CIR concludes Polyisobutene is safe as used in cosmetics.

ingredients.name: Polymethyl Methacrylate
ingredients.note: Spherical acrylic polymer powder used for slip and blur; chemically inert and well tolerated on skin.

ingredients.name: Polymethylsilsesquioxane
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone resin powder used for soft-focus and slip; inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polypodium Leucotomos Extract
ingredients.note: Fern-derived polyphenol antioxidant studied for photo-protection and reducing UV-induced oxidative stress. Safe in topical cosmetic use.

ingredients.name: Polypropylene
ingredients.note: Inert thermoplastic polymer used as a bulking/opacifying agent. Large molecular size means negligible dermal absorption; CIR-class safe.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-39
ingredients.note: Cationic conditioning polymer used as an antistatic, film-forming hair/skin conditioner; CIR concluded safe as used, not absorbed.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-51
ingredients.note: Phosphorylcholine-based polymer (Lipidure) used as a biomimetic humectant and film-former. CIR draft assessment of acryloyloxyethyl phosphorylcholine polymers indicates no risk at current use levels.

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-67
ingredients.note: Cationic, hydrophobically-modified cellulose polymer used as a film-forming, antistatic hair-conditioning agent for softness and manageability.

sources.label: CIR GREEN Safety Assessment of Polyquaterniums as Used in Cosmetics

ingredients.name: Polyquaternium-7
ingredients.note: Cationic conditioning polymer/film former. Safe as used; finished polymer is non-irritating with controlled residual monomers.

ingredients.name: Polysilicone-11
ingredients.note: Crosslinked silicone film former used for long-wear pigment hold in makeup; inert and well tolerated.

ingredients.name: Polysilicone-15
ingredients.note: Siloxane-backbone UVB absorber. SCCNFP found it safe up to 10% with very low (2%) dermal absorption and no sensitization or photoallergenicity.

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 20
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated sorbitan ester used to solubilize fragrance/oils in water. Long history of safe use; ethoxylation means trace 1,4-dioxane is possible but purified grades control it.

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 60
ingredients.note: Ethoxylated sorbitan ester emulsifier for lotions and creams. Regarded as safe; like other ethoxylates may carry trace 1,4-dioxane controlled by purification.

ingredients.name: Polysorbate 80
ingredients.note: Widely used nonionic emulsifier and solubilizer. Safe in cosmetics; ethoxylated process can leave trace 1,4-dioxane that reputable suppliers strip out.

ingredients.name: Polyurethane-35
ingredients.note: Synthetic polyurethane film-former used for water resistance and wear; CIR found polyurethanes safe as used, with only reversible mild eye irritation. Benign.

ingredients.name: Polyvinyl Alcohol
ingredients.note: Synthetic polymer used as a binder, film former and viscosity agent (notably peel-off masks). CIR concluded it is safe as used; generally non-irritating.

ingredients.name: Portulaca Oleracea Extract
ingredients.note: Purslane extract used as a soothing, antioxidant-rich skin-conditioning botanical. Standard well-tolerated plant extract.

ingredients.name: Portulaca Pilosa Extract
ingredients.note: Purslane-relative extract rich in hydrating polysaccharides and antioxidants (quercetin, rutin), used as a soothing humectant skin conditioner. Benign.

PMC: Polysaccharides from Portulaca oleracea – biological activity and application

ingredients.name: Potassium Benzoate
ingredients.note: Potassium salt of benzoic acid used as a preservative. CIR/EU-reviewed as safe at regulated levels.

ingredients.name: Potassium Cetyl Phosphate
ingredients.note: An alkyl phosphate O/W emulsifier; CIR found it safe when formulated to be non-irritating. May irritate eyes at high concentration in concentrate form.

ingredients.name: Potassium Chloride
ingredients.note: Common inorganic salt used as a viscosity controller and electrolyte; recognized as safe and ubiquitous.

ingredients.name: Potassium Cocoate
aliases: potassium salt of coconut fatty acids
ingredients.note: Potassium soap of coconut fatty acids used as a cleansing surfactant in liquid soaps. Considered safe; alkalinity may mildly irritate at high levels.

ingredients.name: Potassium Cocoyl Glycinate
ingredients.note: Mild amino-acid (glycine) surfactant derived from coconut fatty acids; gentle cleanser well tolerated by sensitive skin.

ingredients.name: Potassium Hyaluronate
ingredients.note: Potassium salt of hyaluronic acid used as a humectant; CIR concluded hyaluronates safe as used.

ingredients.name: Potassium Hydroxide
ingredients.note: Strong base used to neutralize/saponify and adjust pH. Caustic in raw form but used at trace neutralizing levels in finished products.

ingredients.name: Potassium Laurate
ingredients.note: Potassium soap of lauric acid used as a surfactant/cleansing and emulsifying agent; fatty-acid soaps are reviewed as safe for cosmetic use, mildly alkaline.

ingredients.name: Potassium Magnesium Aspartate
ingredients.note: A mineral-amino-acid salt providing potassium and magnesium ions used as a skin-conditioning/energizing agent. Benign electrolyte-type cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Potassium Phosphate
ingredients.note: Inorganic potassium salt of phosphoric acid used as a pH buffer/adjuster. CIR assessed phosphoric acid and its salts as safe as used.

ingredients.name: Potassium Sorbate
ingredients.note: Very well-tolerated food-grade preservative effective against yeast and mold. EU-permitted up to 0.6% (as acid); low allergenicity.

ingredients.name: PPG-5-Ceteth-10 Phosphate
ingredients.note: Alkoxylated cetyl alcohol phosphate ester used as a mild emulsifier, surfactant and cleanser. Reported non-irritating and non-sensitizing at use levels.

ingredients.name: Propane

ingredients.name: Propanediol

ingredients.name: Propyl Alcohol

ingredients.name: Propylene Carbonate

ingredients.name: Propylheptyl Caprylate
ingredients.note: A light synthetic ester emollient providing spreadability and a non-greasy skin feel. Standard, well-tolerated cosmetic ingredient.

ingredients.name: Pycnogenol

ingredients.name: Pyridoxine

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fiber

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Extract

ingredients.name: Pyrus Malus (Apple) Fruit Water

ingredients.name: Quercetin
ingredients.note: Plant flavonoid antioxidant with anti-inflammatory and free-radical-scavenging activity. Safe at topical cosmetic levels.
